title: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC Urges Mereo BioPharma Group plc Investors to Act: Class Action Filed Alleging Investor Harm
summary: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC has filed a class action lawsuit against Mereo BioPharma Group plc (NASDAQ: MREO) on behalf of investors who purchased securities between June 5, 2023, and December 26, 2025. The lawsuit alleges that Mereo made misleading statements about its Phase 3 ORBIT and COSMIC programs, which failed to meet primary endpoints. Investors who suffered losses are encouraged to join the case by April 6, 2026, and representation is offered on a contingency fee basis.

title: Citizens reiterates NexPoint Residential Trust stock rating at Market Perform By Investing.com
summary: Citizens has reaffirmed its "Market Perform" rating for NexPoint Residential Trust (NXRT) following the company's Q4 2025 results. Despite operational challenges and a forecasted decline in FFO per share for 2026 primarily due to higher interest expenses, the REIT maintains an attractive 7.89% dividend yield and has consistently raised its dividend for 11 years. Citizens views the shares as fairly valued given the company’s lack of scale and capital structure, while InvestingPro analysis suggests the stock is currently undervalued with analyst targets up to $35.

title: The D. E. Shaw Group Releases Open Letter to the Board of Directors of CoStar Group
summary: The D. E. Shaw Group sent an open letter to CoStar Group's Board of Directors, criticizing the company's decision to reconfigure its reporting segments, which reduces transparency on the Homes.com business's financial performance. This move, following previous concerns about Homes.com's underperformance, is seen by D. E. Shaw as an attempt to evade accountability and has led to significant shareholder value destruction and a decline in CoStar's stock price. The letter urges for improved oversight and transparency to maximize shareholder value.

title: ANGI Energy Unveils Updated Visual Identity as Alternative Fuels Accelerate in Fleet Industry
summary: ANGI Energy Systems, a Vontier company, has unveiled an updated visual identity to reflect its evolution from an equipment supplier to an end-to-end solutions partner for fleet decarbonization. The rebrand emphasizes the company's strategic focus on compressed natural gas (CNG) and renewable natural gas (RNG) as core growth priorities, supported by growing commercial momentum and favorable market conditions for alternative fuels. The refreshed identity aims to reinforce ANGI Energy's role in helping fleet operators transition to low-carbon systems and reduce emissions.

title: Bunge Outlines Strategic Growth and Value Creation Plan at 2026 Investor Day
summary: Bunge Global SA detailed its "Origins to Opportunities" strategic growth plan at its 2026 Investor Day, aiming to become the premier agribusiness solutions company. The company updated its EPS mid-cycle baseline to approximately $13, with an expected increase to at least $15 by the end of 2030, and announced a new $3 billion share repurchase program. Bunge also committed to returning a minimum of 50% of discretionary cash flow to shareholders through dividends and repurchases.

title: Inside the $5B chip lab where Applied and Micron race for faster AI memory
summary: Applied Materials and Micron Technology are partnering to co-develop next-generation DRAM, high-bandwidth memory (HBM), and NAND storage for energy-efficient AI systems. This collaboration will leverage Applied's new $5 billion EPIC Center in Silicon Valley and Micron's R&D hub in Boise, Idaho, aiming to accelerate the commercialization of advanced memory solutions and strengthen U.S. leadership in semiconductor innovation. The initiative focuses on materials engineering, process technologies, and advanced packaging to deliver high-performance, low-power memory essential for future AI workloads.

title: Legal & General Group Plc Increases Adobe Inc. Stake

(NASDAQ:ADBE) by 1.6% during the third quarter of 2026, acquiring an additional 48,578 shares, which brings their total holdings to 3,142,035 shares valued at over $1.1 billion. This move signals continued institutional confidence in Adobe's long-term prospects, particularly its transition to cloud-based and AI-driven products, despite recent market volatility. The investment firm now owns approximately 0.75% of Adobe's outstanding shares.

title: Leidos, Dropzone AI to accelerate federal agencies' cyber responses
summary: Leidos is partnering with Dropzone AI to enhance U.S. federal agencies' cyber response capabilities by deploying AI-driven solutions to manage routine cyber investigations. This collaboration leverages Second Front Systems' Game Warden platform to securely and rapidly integrate Dropzone AI's commercial defensive AI agents into government security operations centers. The goal is to reduce analyst fatigue, improve response times, and address the growing threat landscape exacerbated by AI-assisted attacks, allowing human analysts to concentrate on complex threats.

title: UNFI Q2 2026 Earnings: $7.95B Revenue & Annual Forecast - News and Statistics
summary: United Natural Foods Inc. (UNFI) reported its fiscal second-quarter earnings, announcing a net income of $20 million, or 31 cents per share, with adjusted earnings of 62 cents per share. The company achieved $7.95 billion in revenue for the quarter. UNFI also provided its full-year forecast, expecting earnings between $2.30 and $2.70 per share and revenue ranging from $31 billion to $31.4 billion.

title: Valvoline Inc. launches Happy to Help Fund
summary: Valvoline Inc. has partnered with the Blue Grass Community Foundation to establish the Valvoline Inc. Happy to Help Fund in Lexington, Kentucky. This fund aims to support the mental and physical well-being of children and youth by providing financial assistance to new and existing programs. Valvoline Inc. is committing to address challenges faced by young people, including poverty, food insecurity, and trauma.

title: New Bluetooth tool tracks crowded Wi‑Fi and smart‑home signals at once
summary: Teledyne LeCroy has launched the Frontline X700 Wireless Protocol Analyzer, an advanced system designed to provide comprehensive visibility into Bluetooth and Wi-Fi 7 operations across multiple frequency bands. This tool allows for simultaneous capture of 880 MHz bandwidth, crucial for analyzing highly congested wireless environments used by IoT, smart-home, and mobile devices. It aims to help engineers accelerate product development, improve reliability, and resolve coexistence issues in increasingly complex RF settings.

title: Wall Street's Most Accurate Analysts Give Their Take On 3 Utilities Stocks Delivering High-Dividend Yields
summary: The article highlights three high-dividend-yielding utility stocks—WEC Energy Group Inc (WEC), Exelon Corp (EXC), and Evergy Inc (EVRG)—and provides recent analyst ratings and price targets for each. It notes that investors often turn to such stocks during market turbulence due to their high free cash flows and reliable dividend payouts. The piece includes details from accurate analysts regarding their updated ratings, price targets, and recent news for each company.

title: How a new PPG ship coating method cuts waste and carbon
summary: PPG has completed its 200th vessel dry docking using electrostatic application of marine fouling control coatings, a method that significantly reduces waste and carbon emissions. This technique, applied to the Stena Britannica, utilizes PPG SIGMAGLIDE® 2390 biocide-free coating, improving paint transfer efficiency and lowering material consumption compared to traditional methods. The innovation helps ship owners like Stena Line meet ambitious CO2 reduction targets and contributes to Scope 1 and Scope 3 carbon life cycle savings.

title: Telsey Advisory Lifts PT on Costco Wholesale Corporation (COST) to $1,125 From $1,100 - Here's Why

title: Medtronic to buy Scientia Vascular in $550 million deal
summary: Medtronic announced its agreement to acquire U.S.-based Scientia Vascular for $550 million to expand its product line for stroke care. Scientia Vascular, known for its guidewires and catheters used in brain blood vessels, will enhance Medtronic's neurovascular offerings. Medtronic anticipates the deal to close in the first half of fiscal 2027, with minimal dilution to adjusted earnings in 2027 and accretion thereafter.

title: Headlines Bring Perspective… Actions Capture Opportunity - Raymond James - Commentaries

title: Rackspace–Uniphore AI deal cuts pilot-to-production time to weeks
summary: Rackspace Technology (NASDAQ: RXT) and Uniphore have announced a strategic partnership to deliver the industry's first Infrastructure-to-Agents architecture as an outcomes-based service, targeting $100 million in enterprise AI deployments. This collaboration aims to accelerate AI adoption in regulated industries by combining Uniphore’s Business AI Cloud with Rackspace’s private cloud, offering a secure and governed full-stack AI solution. title: Full-body VR developer Virtuix adds veteran tech executive Brett Moyer to board

Moyer brings extensive public company leadership, capital markets, M&A, and operating experience, having served as CFO of Datavault AI and CEO of WiSA Technologies. This appointment strengthens Virtuix's board as the company expands into defense applications and commercializes its Omni One system, supported by its recent Nasdaq listing and participation in Meta's Made for Meta program.

As a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company, Immunic focuses on developing therapies for chronic inflammatory and autoimmune diseases, currently operating without revenue but with a positive analyst outlook despite financial health warnings.

title: Annovis Bio to Present Buntanetap Clinical Data at AD/PD 2026 Conference

The presentations will cover clinical data from Parkinson’s disease studies, including biomarker findings, and an update on the ongoing Phase 3 trial for early-stage Alzheimer’s disease. Cheng Fang, SVP of R&D, will deliver the presentations, highlighting progress in trial enrollment and supporting biomarker data.

title: How three optometry students won National Vision’s tech-in-eye-care scholarships

The program aims to support the next generation of optometrists in driving innovation within the eye care industry.

title: National Vision Announces Winners of 11th Annual Scholarship Program

title: IonQ and ARLIS Partner to Establish Zero Trust Security Framework for Mission-Critical Quantum Architectures
summary: IonQ announced a collaboration with the Applied Research Laboratory for Intelligence and Security (ARLIS) on the SEQCURE program to develop a Zero Trust Architecture (ZTA) for quantum computing security. This partnership aims to transition quantum security from static perimeters to continuous verification across hardware, software, and networking using NIST standards. The initiative will help ensure secure deployment of quantum technologies for federal agencies and contribute to a more trusted quantum ecosystem.

title: IonQ Tripled Revenue but Its Stock Is Down 22%

The quantum computing company became the first in its sector to exceed $100 million in annual GAAP revenue, but widening EBITDA losses—projected at -$310M to -$330M for 2026—and a high price-to-sales ratio of 100-120 are contributing to investor skepticism and a disconnect between its business performance and stock valuation. Social sentiment is divided, with some investors bullish on long-term potential, while others are concerned about accelerating losses and reliance on government contracts.

The offering includes generative AI tools, audio optimization, and accent robustness to ensure reliable performance in challenging industrial environments, marking Cerence's strategic expansion beyond the automotive sector.

title: Dario Demonstrates Clinically Meaningful Blood Glucose Improvements and Personalized Glycemic Trajectories Across 22,000+ Users: Machine Learning Study Findings Published in Frontiers in Digital Health
summary: DarioHealth Corp. announced the publication of a peer-reviewed study in Frontiers in Digital Health, showcasing substantial and sustained blood glucose improvements among over 22,000 users with type 2 diabetes. The study utilized machine learning to analyze real-world data, revealing that higher digital engagement, particularly frequent blood glucose monitoring (12+ times/month), led to stronger glycemic improvements. These findings emphasize that engagement acts as a clinical signal, enabling personalized and adaptive strategies for effective long-term diabetes management.

title: Global cancer drug rights: Erasca takes ERAS-0015 worldwide

The company will make a one-time payment to Joyo and plans a unified global development strategy for ERAS-0015, which has shown promising early clinical activity across various tumor types. Initial Phase 1 monotherapy data for ERAS-0015 are anticipated in the first half of 2026.

title: War In The Middle East: Economic Implications - Northern Trust - Commentaries
summary: A recent escalation in the Middle East, involving attacks between Israel, the U.S., and Iran, is projected to have significant economic repercussions, primarily through energy markets. The conflict has led to shipping lane closures and reduced LNG production, drastically increasing oil and natural gas prices and impacting global supply chains. Countries in Asia, heavily reliant on the Strait of Hormuz for energy, are particularly vulnerable, facing potential stagflation risks and hindrances to economic growth.

title: WeRide and Farizon expand partnership to scale robotaxi production
summary: WeRide and Farizon New Energy Commercial Vehicle Group have deepened their partnership to accelerate global robotaxi production, introducing an upgraded, production-ready Robotaxi GXR. They aim to deliver approximately 2,000 vehicles by 2026, with mass production of the GXR starting in Q3 2026, and project their operational robotaxi fleet to exceed 2,600 vehicles this year with a long-term goal of tens of thousands globally by 2030. The upgraded GXR features WeRide’s GEN8 autonomous driving system and Farizon’s AI-enabled drive-by-wire chassis, designed to reduce assembly time and overall vehicle cost.

The GMP batch followed two successful development batches that demonstrated consistency and stability, marking a significant step toward clinical testing and regulatory submissions for their innovative intranasal solutions designed to protect against airborne allergens and viruses.

title: Why Your 60/40 Portfolio Is Failing the Inflation Test—And the "Hard Asset" ETF That Could Fix It

The study showed an acceptable safety and tolerability profile for TLX591-Tx in patients with advanced prostate cancer, leading to an almost 14% rise in the company's stock. Telix plans to present this data to the U.S. FDA to advance the trial in the United States and move into a larger randomized expansion phase.

This expansion aims to help TPO partners address complex borrower scenarios and broaden their market reach.

title: Ashford CFO to retire
summary: Ashford's Chief Financial Officer and Treasurer, Deric Eubanks, will retire in June after 23 years with the company. He will step down as CFO of Ashford and its REITs, Ashford Hospitality Trust and Braemar Hotels & Resorts, effective March 31, and will continue as an advisor until June 30 to ensure a smooth transition. Justin Coe, the current chief accounting officer, will succeed him as principal financial officer.

summary: Q32 Bio reported a net income of $29.821 million, driven by $53.737 million in collaboration arrangement revenue and reduced R&D expenses, a significant improvement from the prior year's net loss. Q32 Bio expects its cash position and future funding to support operations into Q4 2027, backed by clinical progress in bempikibart and strategic restructuring.

title: Are Massachusetts Walmart Stores Open on Easter 2026?
summary: This article confirms that Walmart stores in Massachusetts will be open on Easter Sunday, April 5, 2026, despite many other stores being closed. It advises shoppers that while general necessities will be available, Easter-specific items might be out of stock if purchased last minute. The author encourages early preparation for Easter, especially for non-perishable items, given the late date of the holiday this year.

title: Cadence Bank $5.25M MOVEit Data Breach Class Action Settlement
summary: Cadence Bank has agreed to a $5.25 million settlement for a class action lawsuit stemming from a MOVEit data breach in May 2023 that potentially exposed data for 869,411 individuals. Affected customers who received a notification can claim up to $12,500 for documented losses, an estimated $100 pro rata cash payment, and two years of credit monitoring. The deadline to file a claim is June 4, 2026.

The Calgary-based company plans to use $500 million of the proceeds to reduce debt and the remainder to develop infrastructure in northeast British Columbia. This divestiture follows a reported $655 million net loss in the fourth quarter of 2025, despite record production.

title: Markel adds Jamie Smith as London Underwriting Manager – US Cyber Lead

Her nearly two decades of actuarial and analytics experience, including her previous role at Arch Insurance, are expected to strengthen Markel's data-driven decision-making.

Gauzy is seeking to explore all options for its French subsidiaries while emphasizing the continued operations during the observation period.

Concurrently, Gauzy has appointed three new board members, Shlomo Bibas, Michael Martin, and Renah Persofsky, to strengthen governance and support long-term strategic growth. The operations of the French subsidiaries continue during this observation period, and continuation plans remain a possibility.

The designation highlights tegoprubart's potential as a new immunosuppressive treatment, positioning Eledon for growth in its niche market despite inherent biotech sector risks.

title: Eledon Pharma wins FDA orphan status for lead asset (ELDN)

This collaboration leverages Eledon's extensive transplant experience to potentially accelerate development timelines and streamline regulatory processes for NCEL-101.

title: RTX's Pratt & Whitney awarded follow-on contract to supply TJ150 engines for Leidos' small cruise missile
summary: Pratt & Whitney, an RTX business, has secured a follow-on contract from Leidos Dynetics to provide TJ150 turbojet engines for the AGM-190A small cruise missile. The TJ150 is a compact, high-performance engine capable of high-altitude operation, with over 2,700 units already delivered globally across various missile applications. Pratt & Whitney is also integrating additive manufacturing into the TJ150's development to enhance production flexibility, speed, and cost-effectiveness.

title: BofA assumes Qualcomm stock coverage with underperform rating
summary: BofA Securities initiated coverage on Qualcomm (NASDAQ:QCOM) with an Underperform rating and a $145 price target, citing projected sales and earnings growth significantly lower than the broader semiconductor sector. This cautious outlook contrasts with some other analysts who have recently upgraded Qualcomm, recognizing its diversification efforts and potential in new markets.

title: Zacks Industry Outlook Highlights Palo Alto,CrowdStrike, Fortinet and Okta
summary: The Zacks Security industry is experiencing strong demand for cybersecurity products due to increased data breaches and the shift towards digital transformation, benefiting companies like Palo Alto Networks, CrowdStrike, Fortinet, and Okta. Despite macroeconomic headwinds potentially delaying IT investments and increasing operating expenses, the industry shows solid near-term prospects with a positive earnings outlook. Okta, CrowdStrike, and Fortinet are highlighted as strong performers, while Palo Alto Networks is a hold due to revisions in its earnings estimates.

title: Morgan Stanley, eBay job cuts reflect a new kind of workforce reset
summary: Morgan Stanley and eBay have implemented significant job cuts, reflecting a trend of companies restructuring from a position of strength rather than distress. These layoffs occur against a backdrop of mixed labor market signals, with overall job cuts down in February but year-to-date figures remaining elevated. Technology, particularly AI, is increasingly cited as a driver for these workforce reductions.

title: Marathon, Chevron, PBF Warn Governor Newsom of Widespread Refinery Shutdowns, Fuel shortages, Economic Collapse – California Globe
summary: Major oil companies Marathon Petroleum, PBF Energy, and Chevron have issued severe warnings to California Governor Gavin Newsom and state officials, stating that proposed amendments to the California Air Resources Board’s (CARB) Cap-and-Invest program could lead to widespread refinery shutdowns, fuel shortages, and economic collapse. They project significant increases in gasoline prices and the potential closure of vital refineries. The companies argue that these policies are making California’s refineries the most expensive globally to operate and are forcing the state to rely more heavily on foreign oil imports.

title: Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) Units to Pay $65 Million in Tracleer Antitrust Class Action Settlement, Reuters Reports

The lawsuit alleged that these units overcharged for the pulmonary hypertension drug Tracleer and hindered generic competition by obstructing access to drug samples. The preliminary settlement, filed in a Maryland federal court, awaits judicial approval.

title: CHIPOTLE BRINGS BACK "TATTED LIKE A CHIPOTLE BAG" BOGO WITH SUPERFAN SWAE LEE FOR FRIDAY THE 13TH
summary: Chipotle is bringing back its "Tatted Like a Chipotle Bag" buy-one-get-one (BOGO) offer on Friday, March 13, from 3 to 4 p.m. local time, inviting fans with any type of tattoo—permanent, temporary, or drawn-on—to participate. The promotion, inspired by an internet meme and the Friday the 13th flash tattoo tradition, features a partnership with hip-hop artist Swae Lee, who co-created an exclusive temporary tattoo flash sheet available at a Miami Chipotle location. This initiative aims to celebrate Chipotle superfans and connect with the tattoo community, following a highly successful prior BOGO event.

title: The D. E. Shaw Group Releases Open Letter to the Board of Directors of CoStar Group
summary: The D. E. Shaw Group has issued an open letter to the Board of Directors of CoStar Group, Inc., criticizing the company's recent decision to reconfigure its reporting segments, which they argue reduces transparency into CoStar's financial results and, specifically, the underperforming Homes.com business. The investment firm contends that this move is an attempt to evade accountability for Homes.com's continued underperformance and demonstrates a disregard for shareholder interests, especially given the significant drop in CoStar's stock price following the announcement. D. E. Shaw urged the Board to prioritize transparency and accountability to maximize shareholder value.

title: Pimly Announces Product Intelligence on Salesforce AgentExchange
summary: Pimly has launched Product Intelligence on Salesforce AgentExchange, aiming to transform product information management into a dynamic intelligence layer for AI, automation, and front-office growth. The solution, built for manufacturers, retailers, and CPG brands using Salesforce, unifies and activates product data across various Salesforce solutions. This integration is designed to prevent data hallucinations, enable accurate responses, and streamline processes like quoting, ordering, and new product launches for Salesforce customers.

title: CHIPOTLE BRINGS BACK "TATTED LIKE A CHIPOTLE BAG" BOGO WITH SUPERFAN SWAE LEE FOR FRIDAY THE 13TH
summary: Chipotle is bringing back its "Tatted Like a Chipotle Bag" BOGO promotion on Friday, March 13, 2026, from 3 to 4 p.m. local time, offering a buy-one-get-one free deal to customers with any tattoo. title: Markets Are Misreading Fed’s Response to Oil Surge, BofA Says
summary: Bank of America (BofA) analysts suggest that investors may be misinterpreting the Federal Reserve's likely response to rising oil prices. Despite a market expectation of hawkish measures due to the Iran war pushing up oil prices, BofA warns that supply shocks can also lead to stable or even reduced interest rates.

title: Bunge Outlines Strategic Growth and Value Creation Plan at 2026 Investor Day
summary: Bunge Global SA (NYSE: BG) is hosting its 2026 Investor Day to present its strategic growth and value creation plan, including key commercial initiatives and the ongoing integration of Viterra. The company has updated its mid-cycle EPS baseline to approximately $13 and aims to increase it to at least $15 by the end of 2030. Bunge also announced a capital allocation framework targeting a minimum of 50% discretionary cash flow return to shareholders through dividends and a new $3 billion share repurchase plan.

title: Erste Group Initiated Applied Materials, Inc. (AMAT) With a Hold Rating - Here's Why
summary: Erste Group has initiated coverage on Applied Materials, Inc. (NASDAQ: AMAT) with a Hold rating. Applied Materials reported strong Q1 2026 results with $7.01 billion in revenue and generated $1.69 billion in cash from operations, distributing $702 million to shareholders.

title: Applied Materials and Micron Partner To Advance U.S. Innovation in Next-Generation AI Memory Solutions
summary: Applied Materials and Micron Technology are collaborating to develop advanced DRAM, high-bandwidth memory (HBM), and NAND solutions for AI systems. This partnership leverages Applied's EPIC Center in Silicon Valley and Micron's innovation center in Boise, Idaho, aiming to strengthen U.S. leadership in semiconductor innovation and create more energy-efficient AI memory. The initiative will focus on next-generation materials, process technologies, architectures, and advanced packaging to enable high-bandwidth, low-power memory for AI workloads.

title: ADP National Employment Report Preliminary Estimate for February 21, 2026
summary: The ADP National Employment Report (NER) Pulse indicates that U.S. private employers added an average of 15,500 jobs per week for the four weeks ending February 21, 2026. This preliminary estimate shows employment gains holding steady, following five weeks of strengthening. The NER Pulse, produced by ADP Research in collaboration with the Stanford Digital Economy Lab, provides weekly updates on employment trends based on high-frequency, seasonally adjusted data.

title: HIV tests, diabetes tech and a new CGM: inside Trinity Biotech's 2026 overhaul
summary: Trinity Biotech (TRIB) updated on Q4 2025 trading and business progress, projecting Q4 2025 revenue of $11.5M–$12.5M, a decrease from Q4 2024 due to HIV testing market disruptions and manufacturing transitions. Despite this, the company highlighted positive developments such as regulatory approval to outsource Uni-Gold manufacturing, growing demand for its rapid HIV tests, IFCC Gold classification for its HbA1c solution, and ongoing development of new products like CGM+, EpiCapture, and PrePsia. The market reacted positively, with TRIB stock gaining 5.10% following the news, reflecting investor confidence in the company's long-term transformation strategy.

title: GSK Out-Licenses Rights for Rare Liver Disease Drug to Alfasigma
summary: GSK has signed a license agreement with Italy-based Alfasigma, granting them exclusive global rights to develop and market linerixibat, an investigational therapy for cholestatic pruritus in primary biliary cholangitis (PBC). GSK will receive $300 million upfront, with potential for an additional $390 million in milestone payments and tiered double-digit royalties on net sales. Linerixibat previously demonstrated significant improvements in pruritus and sleep interference in the late-stage GLISTEN study, and a final FDA decision is expected by March 24, 2026.

summary: Centene Corporation (CNC) is scheduled to participate in the Barclays 28th Annual Global Healthcare Conference on March 10, 2026, where it will reaffirm its 2026 full-year GAAP diluted EPS guidance of greater than $1.98 and adjusted diluted EPS guidance of greater than $3.00. Additionally, the company announced a partial redemption of $1 billion of its 4.25% Notes due December 15, 2027, with approximately $1.19 billion of these notes remaining outstanding after the March 25, 2026 redemption. title: Ross and dds Discounts Open New Stores in Southern California as Part of 2026 Expansion - News and Statistics
summary: Ross Stores Inc. has opened new Ross Dress for Less and dds Discounts locations in Southern California, specifically in Alhambra and North Hollywood, respectively. This initiative is part of the company's 2026 national expansion strategy, aiming to launch 110 new stores this year, including 85 Ross stores and 25 dds Discounts stores, following strong performance from new openings in the previous year. Ross targets a broad customer base, while dds Discounts focuses on younger, ethnically diverse, low-to-moderate income shoppers.

title: Lockheed Martin Secures $700M F-35 Contract for Partner and Foreign Military Orders
summary: Lockheed Martin has been awarded a $700.4 million modification to an existing contract for the F-35 production aircraft. This funding will procure long-lead materials, parts, and components for Lots 20 and 21 of F-35s destined for Denmark, F-35 Cooperative Program Partners, and Foreign Military Sales customers. The work is expected to be completed by December 2030, with funds obligated from both cooperative program partners and foreign military sales customers.

title: AT&T Is Spending $250B On Connectivity In The US – Here’s Where The Money Is Going
summary: AT&T announced a plan to invest over $250 billion in the next five years to enhance and modernize its communications infrastructure across the U.S. This funding will focus on expanding fiber internet, 5G wireless networks, and satellite-supported connectivity. CEO John Stankey stated the investment aims to increase U.S. connectivity competitiveness and improve access to AT&T's networks in various regions.

title: VIAVI Launches Industry-Leading True-Phase DAS Fiber Sensing Interrogator with AI/ML at the Edge to Enable Real-Time Infrastructure Monitoring
summary: VIAVI Solutions (NASDAQ: VIAV) has introduced a new FTH-DAS (fiber test head for distributed acoustic sensing) interrogator, enhancing their NITRO™ Fiber Sensing solution with embedded AI/ML at the edge. This true-phase DAS interrogator provides real-time infrastructure monitoring, enabling multi-event detection, localization, and classification of threats over long distances. The system uses a patented phase-stepping interferometry technique to recover optical phase from Rayleigh backscatter, allowing true measurements of physical quantities and robust machine learning for enhanced accuracy and immediate alerts.

title: New Viavi fiber sensor uses edge AI to detect leaks, digging, even footsteps
summary: VIAVI Solutions (NASDAQ: VIAV) has launched its FTH-DAS, a new true-phase distributed acoustic sensing interrogator with embedded AI/ML for edge deployment. This device provides real-time multi-event detection, localization, and classification across multiple fibers, leveraging patented phase-stepping interferometry for precise physical measurements. Its on-device AI/ML models enable adaptable infrastructure monitoring for applications such as pipeline leak detection, border surveillance, and critical infrastructure protection.

The system offers flexible panel options, including comprehensive and targeted Mini panels, providing rapid results in about one hour to support timely clinical decisions for infections.

title: Civil Rights Case Probes Racism Behind Cancer Alley Pollution
summary: A federal civil rights lawsuit alleging that St. James Parish officials in Louisiana's "Cancer Alley" illegally steered polluting industries into Black communities reached a new stage after a judge denied a motion to dismiss. The case uniquely links environmental harms to the 13th Amendment's prohibition on "badges and incidents" of slavery, arguing that current zoning practices are a modern manifestation of historic racial discrimination. Plaintiffs, including local environmental justice organizations, are seeking to halt permits for new industrial projects in these communities, protect ancestral burial grounds, and appoint an independent monitor.

This initiative aims to commercialize advanced AI robotics by integrating composite AI, mixed-criticality systems, and a continuous AI data learning cycle within a shared ecosystem.

title: Chinese Telecommunications Company Fined $50 Million for Conspiring to Steal Technology from Motorola Solutions
summary: A federal judge in Chicago has fined Hytera Communications Corp. LTD, a Chinese telecommunications company, $50 million for conspiring to steal proprietary technology from Illinois-based Motorola Solutions, Inc. The company recruited Motorola employees to take trade secrets related to digital mobile radio technology, using the stolen information to develop competing products at a lower cost. Additionally, Hytera was sentenced to five years of probation and ordered to pay approximately $214 million in restitution, offsetting previous civil judgment payments.

title: Emails Show AT&T CEO Raised Crime And Governance Concerns With Dallas Leaders Before HQ Exit
summary: Emails obtained through a public records request reveal that AT&T CEO John Stankey privately expressed concerns about crime and governance in downtown Dallas to city leaders in 2025, months before the company announced its headquarters relocation to Plano. Stankey's correspondence with City Manager Kimberly Bizor Tolbert highlighted "ongoing and cyclical nature of our challenges with effective/sustained governance of the City" and its impact on a healthy business environment. Despite the city's "Safe In the City" campaign, Stankey reiterated that foundational issues needed to be considered for AT&T's long-term decisions, underscoring the reasons behind their eventual move.

title: AI and Economic Moats: Which Stocks Are Most at Risk?

However, some firms like Synopsys and Cloudflare are expected to benefit from AI, and despite increased uncertainty, opportunities for investors still exist among certain undervalued incumbent companies.

title: A Look At Iovance Biotherapeutics (IOVA) Valuation After Sharp Recent Share Price Momentum
