The company is focusing on digital distribution, expanding recurring revenue streams through RaceControl subscriptions, and returning to esports with the Le Mans Virtual Series.

title: Motorsport Games turns $3M loss into $6.8M profit in 2025
summary: Motorsport Games (NASDAQ: MSGM) reported a significant financial turnaround in 2025, moving from a $3.0 million net loss in 2024 to a $6.8 million net profit. The company's revenue increased by 30% year-over-year to $11.3 million, driven primarily by strong sales of its "Le Mans Ultimate" racing title and related DLC. This financial improvement was also supported by a higher gross profit margin and controlled operating expenses, indicating a successful strategic shift towards core racing simulation products.

Shannon, former CFO of Lakeland Industries, Inc., for allegedly overseeing unreliable financial projections before his termination. The stock's current price of $63.12 is near its 52-week high, reflecting a 64.5% increase year-to-date.

title: Esperion Therapeutics, Inc (ESPR) Misses Q4 EPS Estimates
summary: Esperion Therapeutics, Inc (ESPR) reported Q4 2025 earnings of $0.22 per share, missing analyst estimates. The company generated $168.4 million in revenue for the quarter. Esperion Therapeutics focuses on developing and commercializing non-statin medicines for patients with elevated LDL cholesterol, including NEXLETOL and NEXLIZET.

title: CFO shift at ICL Group (NYSE: ICL) as Alperovitz replaces Lahav
summary: ICL Group announced a Chief Financial Officer transition, with Aviram Lahav concluding his tenure on June 15, 2026, ahead of retirement. Asaf Alperovitz, with over two decades of senior financial experience including CFO roles at SolarEdge Technologies, will succeed him. ICL's President and CEO, Elad Aharonson, expressed confidence that Alperovitz's background will support the company's continued growth and strategic execution.

title: loanDepot Announces Year-End and Fourth Quarter 2025 Financial Results
summary: loanDepot (NYSE: LDI) announced its year-end and fourth quarter 2025 financial results, highlighting increased loan origination volume and market share. The company reported a 12% revenue increase year-over-year to $1.19 billion and a 46% increase in Adjusted EBITDA to $122 million for the full year 2025, despite experiencing a net loss. Fourth-quarter highlights include a 23% increase in loan origination volume to $8.04 billion, the highest since 2022, and a 19% increase in market share.

title: First Trust Enhanced Equity Income Fund Declares its Quarterly Distribution of $0.3875 Per Share
summary: The First Trust Enhanced Equity Income Fund (NYSE: FFA) has announced its regular quarterly distribution of $0.3875 per share, payable on March 31, 2026, to shareholders of record as of March 20, 2026. The Fund operates under a managed distribution policy to pay out long-term capital gains more frequently, and its objective is to provide a high level of current income and gains, and capital appreciation through a diversified portfolio of equity securities. First Trust Advisors L.P. serves as the investment advisor, with Chartwell Investment Partners, LLC as the investment sub-advisor.

title: ExxonMobil board recommends moving corporate registration to Texas
summary: ExxonMobil's board of directors has recommended moving the company's corporate registration from New Jersey to Texas, aligning its legal domicile with its physical headquarters in Spring, north of Houston. CEO Darren Woods stated this move reflects Texas's business-friendly environment and its understanding of the company's operations, aiming to maximize shareholder value. Governor Greg Abbott lauded the decision, emphasizing Texas's role as a growing corporate hub.

title: Equities fall as investors shift to fixed income amid valuation worries, State Street Corporation cautions
summary: State Street Corporation reported a modest positive movement in its Institutional Investor Indicators for February. However, equity markets declined as investors favored fixed income assets. This shift is attributed to concerns over high equity valuations and uncertainty surrounding artificial intelligence trends, as highlighted by Lee Ferridge, head of Macro Strategy at State Street.

title: Verisk Analytics Touts Data Advantage at FinTech Forum
summary: Verisk Analytics, a prominent data analytics firm, highlighted its significant data assets and long-term financial targets at the Wolfe Research FinTech Forum. CFO Elizabeth Mann emphasized the company's leading data sets as crucial for training AI models, positioning Verisk as a key player in the evolving financial technology sector. The company views AI as an opportunity, leveraging its data-driven approach and operational strength to maintain a competitive advantage.

title: Teradyne sues Chinese cobot maker over UR software
summary: Teradyne Robotics, parent company of Universal Robots (UR), is suing Elite Robots, a Chinese collaborative robot maker, for alleged infringement of UR's proprietary software in German courts. Teradyne is emphasizing the need for stronger action against intellectual property violations, arguing that unchecked infringement harms European innovators and potentially compromises customer experience and safety standards. This lawsuit occurs amid increasing concern over Chinese competition in industrial robotics and follows Teradyne's internal challenges including declining UR revenue and workforce reductions, even as it continues to invest in its robotics group.

title: Texas Pacific Land, Cactus, and BKV Shares Plummet, What You Need To Know
summary: Shares of Texas Pacific Land (TPL), Cactus (WHD), and BKV (BKV) plunged following a sharp decline in crude oil and natural gas prices. This downturn was attributed to shifting geopolitical expectations, including comments from President Trump suggesting a brief war with Iran and global leaders signaling intervention in energy markets. Despite the significant drops, the article suggests that these price retreats could present buying opportunities for high-quality stocks.

title: Key facts: Ulta Beauty Inc. forecasts strong 2026 sales; price target raised
summary: Ulta Beauty Inc. is projected to achieve strong sales in 2026, primarily driven by the launch of Rare Beauty, which is expected to boost customer traffic and overall sales. Analysts, including Canaccord Genuity, note improved profitability from Ulta's product pipeline, leading Canaccord Genuity to raise its price target for ULTA to $799.00 per share.

title: PNC's Nashville growth plans: 'We want you to see PNC everywhere'
summary: PNC Financial Services Group plans to significantly expand its presence in Nashville, increasing its branch count from six to 41 by 2030. The company anticipates 2026 will be its most successful year to date in the region, demonstrating strong momentum for its growth strategy in Music City. Mike D. Johnson, regional president, highlighted the bank's ambition to be widely visible.

title: Occupancy Remains Strong at 96% and NOI Growth Remains Solid for Simon's Class A Mall Portfolio
summary: This article highlights that Simon Property Group's Class A mall portfolio continues to demonstrate strong performance with a 96% occupancy rate and solid Net Operating Income (NOI) growth. The company, the largest mall REIT, manages a high-quality retail portfolio sought after by retailers for its unique shopping experiences in dense, high-income markets. Analyst Kevin Brown also references previous strong tenant sales growth and strategic acquisitions by Simon Property Group.

title: Paycom Releases Automated Career and Succession Planning Tool
summary: Paycom Software, Inc. (NYSE: PAYC) has launched a new automated Career and Succession Planning tool designed to help organizations identify successors, assess bench strength, and manage talent development. This tool integrates with existing employee data, offering a unified visual dashboard, analytics, and talent mapping. It aims to eliminate guesswork in talent planning by using various data points to match employees with future roles.

title: Brokers Suggest Investing in Leidos (LDOS): Read This Before Placing a Bet
summary: This article analyzes broker recommendations for Leidos (LDOS), which currently holds an average brokerage recommendation (ABR) of 1.94, suggesting a "Buy." However, it cautions investors against relying solely on ABRs due to inherent positive bias from brokerage firms, advocating instead for validation with research tools like the Zacks Rank. For LDOS, the Zacks Rank is #4 (Sell) despite the favorable ABR, due to declining earnings estimates, suggesting investors should approach the positive ABR with skepticism.

title: Leidos to participate in the 2026 J.P. Morgan Industrials Conference
summary: Leidos (NYSE: LDOS) announced its participation in J.P. Morgan's 2026 Industrials Conference, to be held in Washington, D.C. A live audio webcast of their presentation will be available on March 17, 2026, from 1:40 p.m. to 2:15 p.m. ET. The announcement was made via PR Newswire on March 10, 2026.

title: Keysight Powers the Future of E-Mobility With Test Solutions for High-Power and Megawatt Charging
summary: Keysight Technologies has introduced two new electric vehicle (EV) charging test solutions designed to support high-power and megawatt-level charging for accelerating e-mobility. These solutions, the SL2600A Megawatt Charging Discovery System and the enhanced SL1047A Scienlab Charging Discovery System – High‑Power Series, aim to help manufacturers and engineers speed up development, ensure reliability, and meet evolving global standards for EV charging infrastructure. The systems offer flexibility, scalability, and compliance with various international charging standards, addressing challenges such as interoperability and safety in the rapidly growing EV market.

title: JPMorgan Chase Had A Rough 2025 (And Its Still Paying For It)
summary: JPMorgan Chase (JPM) experienced a decrease in net income in 2025 by 2.6% to $57 billion, due to factors like lowered credit card rates and setting aside $2.2 billion for Apple Card reserves. Despite some growth in new accounts, CEO Jamie Dimon expressed caution for 2026 due to geopolitical conditions and sticky inflation. The article also highlights President Trump's proposed 10% interest rate cap on credit cards as a potential hazard for banks and mentions the bank's strong 2025 stock performance could set a high bar for future expectations.

title: 2 Growth Stocks to Hold for the Next Decade
summary: This article identifies Intuitive Surgical and DexCom as two growth stocks with strong long-term potential, despite recent headwinds. Intuitive Surgical, a leader in robotic-assisted surgery, is expected to benefit from an aging global population and its wide economic moat. DexCom, a continuous glucose monitoring device company, has significant growth opportunities in an underpenetrated market and through new product launches, including those targeting non-diabetic individuals.

title: Guarantor: JPMorgan Chase & Co.
summary: This document is a pricing supplement for $714,000 Capped Buffered Return Enhanced Notes linked to the S&P 500 Index, issued by JPMorgan Chase Financial Company LLC and fully guaranteed by JPMorgan Chase & Co. The notes offer investors 1.33 times the appreciation of the S&P 500 Index, up to a maximum return of 30.00%, but investors can lose up to 80.00% of their principal. The document details the investment terms, risk considerations, hypothetical payout profiles, and tax treatment related to these structured investments.

title: Why Intuit Inc Stock Fell Despite a Fresh Upgrade as Tax Season Heats Up
summary: Intuit (NASDAQ: INTU) stock dropped 3.9% despite a "buy" rating and raised price target from Rothschild & Co Redburn, which highlighted QuickBooks and TurboTax’s resilience against AI headwinds. The decline occurred as the company prepares for the April 15 tax deadline, even after reporting strong January quarter results with 17% revenue growth and positive adjusted earnings. Investors are seeking firmer evidence of real profit gains rather than just revenue boosts from tax season and AI partnerships to alleviate concerns about competition and AI disruption in the software sector.

title: Invesco Ltd. Announces February 28, 2026 Assets Under Management
summary: Invesco Ltd. (NYSE: IVZ) announced its preliminary month-end assets under management (AUM) for February 28, 2026, reaching $2,257.7 billion, a 1.2% increase from the previous month. The firm experienced net long-term inflows of $4.7 billion and money market net inflows of $13.5 billion. Favorable market returns positively impacted AUM by $9 billion, while FX decreased it by $1.1 billion.

title: Key facts: Humana Inc. raises $1 billion; to acquire Homsan AB
summary: Humana Inc. successfully raised $1.0 billion through a public offering of 6.625% Junior Subordinated Notes due 2056, allocating approximately $986 million for corporate needs including debt repayment. The company also announced its acquisition of Homsan AB, a move expected to significantly expand its services in Stockholm by increasing participant numbers from 160 to 360. Homsan, operational since 2010, reports annual sales of about SEK 63 million.

title: Hewlett Packard Enterprise, Sinclair, Vestis, First Advantage, and Insight Enterprises Shares Plummet, What You Need To Know
summary: Shares of Hewlett Packard Enterprise, Sinclair, Vestis, First Advantage, and Insight Enterprises plummeted due to geopolitical instability in the Middle East, which caused crude oil prices to fluctuate and created a volatile market. The market's reaction highlights its sensitivity to the conflict, with investors showing risk-aversion. While the overall market was affected, each company's specific situation, like Sinclair's volatile stock and recent earnings beat despite revenue decline, played a role in investor perception.

title: Hasbro CEO uses AI at home — but won't let it touch D&D or Magic
summary: Hasbro CEO Chris Cocks has assured that AI tools will not be used for creating content for Dungeons & Dragons and Magic: The Gathering, despite their extensive use in other Hasbro product design. Cocks revealed that while AI significantly speeds up product development for lines like Peppa Pig and Transformers, he also personally uses AI for his D&D "personal passion projects." The decision to keep AI out of D&D and Magic stems from creator and audience preferences.

title: PECO Reinforces Commitment to Fight Rising Energy Costs as Part of ‘The Exelon Promise’
summary: PECO is reinforcing its commitment to fighting rising energy costs through "The Exelon Promise," a comprehensive strategy to deliver immediate relief and long-term solutions for customers in southeastern Pennsylvania. This initiative addresses increasing energy supply prices due to growing demand and market dynamics by offering customer support, implementing protections against unfair cost impacts from large energy users, and advocating for reforms like expanding long-term supply contracts and allowing utilities to generate affordable power. PECO's efforts include a $12.5 million Customer Relief Fund and supporting legislation to ensure Pennsylvania controls its energy future.

title: First Solar, Inc. (FSLR) Gains Analyst Support as Strong Demand Boosts Solar Growth Outlook
summary: First Solar, Inc. (FSLR) has received continued analyst support despite a price target cut, as strong demand for domestically sourced solar modules is expected to boost its prospects. The company reported solid fourth-quarter and full-year results, with net sales increasing to $1.7 billion in Q4 and $5.2 billion for the full year, driven by higher module volume. First Solar, a leading U.S. manufacturer of PV solar modules, is also anticipated to benefit from a 10% domestic content adder due to strong customer demand.

title: Diamondback Energy Announces Launch of Secondary Common Stock Offering
summary: Diamondback Energy, Inc. announced the launch of an underwritten public offering of 11,000,000 shares of its common stock by SGF FANG Holdings, LP. Diamondback will not receive any proceeds from this secondary offering, and the selling stockholder has granted underwriters an option to purchase additional shares to cover over-allotments. Evercore ISI, Citigroup, and J.P. Morgan are acting as joint book-running managers.

title: Encompass Health SVP Fay sells $1.27 million in stock
summary: Edmund Fay, SVP and Treasurer at Encompass Health Corp (NYSE:EHC), sold shares worth $1.27 million and donated an additional 3,000 shares to a donor advised fund on March 9, 2026. Following these transactions, Fay directly owns 86,325 shares. This news follows Encompass Health's strong Q4 2025 earnings, which surpassed analyst expectations, and positive reiterations of "Buy" or "Strong Buy" ratings from Raymond James and Truist Securities.

title: CSX-served transformer plant launches $200 million expansion
summary: HD Hyundai Power Transformers USA, a CSX-served facility in Montgomery, Ala., has begun a $200 million expansion. This project, expected to be completed by mid-2027, will allow the company to manufacture larger electrical transformers and support 600 jobs. CSX is collaborating with HD-HPT to manage the logistics of moving complex, oversized loads, strengthening the supply chain and supporting regional growth.

title: Salesforce confirms ShinyHunters exploited Experience Cloud sites
summary: Salesforce confirmed that the ShinyHunters cybercrime group exploited misconfigured Salesforce Experience Cloud sites. The company emphasizes this is a customer-configured guest user setting issue, not a platform vulnerability, affecting hundreds of organizations. Security experts advise companies to audit guest user profiles, disable public API access, and implement zero-trust principles for SaaS platforms.

title: Have tattoos? This Mexican-style chain is offering up to 5 free meals for ink of all kinds
summary: Chipotle is bringing back its "Tatted Like a Chipotle Bag" buy-one-get-one-free (BOGO) entree promotion on Friday, March 13, for tattooed customers. The deal, valid for one hour between 3 p.m. and 4 p.m. in the U.S. and Canada, allows customers to get up to five free menu items. Tattoos can be real, temporary, or drawn-on, giving everyone a chance to participate.

title: Copart, Huron, Brown & Brown, Marsh & McLennan, and Omnicom Group Shares Are Falling, What You Need To Know
summary: Shares of several companies including Copart, Huron, Brown & Brown, Marsh & McLennan, and Omnicom Group experienced declines due to heightened geopolitical tensions and broader economic uncertainty, particularly related to the Middle East conflict. This market anxiety has led corporations to reduce spending and impacted the business services sector, with concerns also arising from a global trend of worsening late payments from buyers. Copart's specific drop is noted in the context of its recent underperformance in Q4 financial results, missing revenue and earnings estimates.

title: Form 424B2 CITIGROUP INC
summary: Citigroup Global Markets Holdings Inc. is offering Callable Contingent Coupon Equity Linked Securities linked to the Dow Jones Industrial Average, Nasdaq-100 Index, and S&P 500 Index, due September 11, 2028. These unsecured debt securities, guaranteed by Citigroup Inc., offer a potential higher yield through periodic contingent coupon payments but carry significant risks, including the possibility of receiving less than the principal amount or no coupon payments if any underlying index performs poorly. The securities are subject to market volatility, credit risk of the issuers, and complex tax considerations.

title: Form 424B2 CITIGROUP INC
summary: This SEC filing (Form 424B2) from Citigroup Global Markets Holdings Inc., guaranteed by Citigroup Inc., details the offering of Callable Contingent Coupon Equity Linked Securities due March 9, 2028. These securities are linked to the performance of the Nasdaq-100 Index, Russell 2000 Index, and S&P 500 Index, offering potential periodic contingent coupon payments that could be higher than conventional debt but carry significant risks, including the potential loss of principal. The document provides key terms, hypothetical examples of payments, and a comprehensive summary of associated risks for investors.

title: Copart, Huron, Brown & Brown, Marsh & McLennan, and Omnicom Group Shares Are Falling, What You Need To Know
summary: Shares of Copart, Huron, Brown & Brown, Marsh & McLennan, and Omnicom Group experienced declines in the afternoon session due to investor concerns over heightened geopolitical tensions and broader economic uncertainty, particularly related to the Middle East conflict. This market anxiety is leading companies to reduce spending, affecting the business services sector and impacting cash flow for providers. Copart specifically saw a 3% drop, following recent disappointing fourth-quarter financial results that missed revenue and earnings expectations.

title: 3 Reasons to Avoid BK and 1 Stock to Buy Instead
summary: This article explains why investors should avoid Bank of New York Mellon Corp (BK) despite its recent market outperformance. The author cites BK's disappointing long-term revenue growth, only modest growth in tangible book value per share, and uninspiring return on equity as reasons to be cautious. Instead, the author recommends considering an unnamed "safe-and-steady industrials business benefiting from an upgrade cycle" and refers to a list of top growth stocks flagged by AI.

title: West Virginia Public Service Commission Authorizes New Rates for West Virginia American Water
summary: The West Virginia Public Service Commission has approved new rates for West Virginia American Water, resulting in a $20.5 million annualized revenue increase, excluding previously recovered infrastructure surcharges. This authorization supports over $239 million in continued infrastructure investments across the state and introduces an additional customer discount program. The new rates were approved based on an amended order issued on March 5, 2026.

title: Guarantor: JPMorgan Chase & Co.
summary: JPMorgan Chase Financial Company LLC has issued $1,452,000 Uncapped Dual Directional Digital Barrier Notes, fully and unconditionally guaranteed by JPMorgan Chase & Co. These structured investments are linked to the least performing of the Dow Jones Industrial Average, Nasdaq-100 Index, and S&P 500 Index, maturing on March 9, 2029. The notes offer investors uncapped, unleveraged exposure to index appreciation with a contingent minimum return of 33.00%, or a capped return on depreciation up to 25.00% if certain barrier conditions are met, but involve risks including potential loss of principal.

title: How Procter & Gamble got ‘good buzz’ by briefly retiring Mr. Clean
summary: Procter & Gamble's Mr. Clean brand generated "good buzz" by orchestrating a social media-driven retirement and comeback campaign for its mascot, Mr. Clean. Spearheaded by Publicis shop MSL, the effort aimed to refresh the brand and introduce new product innovations, despite the challenge of making cleaning products appealing. The campaign successfully garnered significant earned media attention and consumer engagement by treating the mascot's activities and eventual return as a compelling narrative.

title: NextEra Energy avoided paying all of $2.5 million fee for Enid solar farm
summary: NextEra Energy negotiated down a $2.5 million construction permit fee for its Skeleton Creek solar farm near Enid, Oklahoma, ultimately paying $500,000. An investigation by Investigate Midwest, using an open records request, revealed the details of the negotiations and involvement of the State Attorney General's office. This project is part of NextEra's significant investment in renewable energy in Oklahoma, despite initial resident opposition.

title: 3 Reasons to Sell WST and 1 Stock to Buy Instead
summary: This article recommends selling West Pharmaceutical Services (WST) due to its mediocre long-term revenue growth, shrinking adjusted operating margin, and declining return on invested capital (ROIC). The author suggests better opportunities exist elsewhere, and recommends a top digital advertising platform riding the creator economy instead.

title: Salesforce confirms ShinyHunters exploited Experience Cloud sites
summary: Salesforce has confirmed that the ShinyHunters cybercrime group exploited misconfigured Experience Cloud sites, not a platform vulnerability. This incident affected hundreds of organizations due to overly broad guest user permissions. Attackers used a weaponized Aura Inspector tool to scan for and exploit these misconfigurations, prompting Salesforce to urge customers to audit permissions and disable public API access for guest users.

title: Elevance expands policy penalizing out-of-network referrals
summary: Elevance Health is broadening a policy to penalize in-network hospitals that refer their members to out-of-network healthcare providers. This expansion aims to control costs by discouraging patient referrals outside of its preferred network.

title: This Is Why CRISPR Therapeutics Stock Is Tumbling on Tuesday
summary: CRISPR Therapeutics' stock plummeted over 12% today after the company announced its intention to issue $350 million worth of convertible notes to raise funds. While the market reacted negatively to the potential for shareholder dilution, this fundraising is typical for biotech startups and is expected to fuel the development of CRISPR's promising gene-editing drug pipeline, which includes an already approved gene therapy and five ongoing clinical trials. Analysts maintain a bullish stance on CRSP, suggesting this dip could be a buying opportunity for risk-tolerant investors.

title: Taser Company to Disclose Political Spending, Ending Lawsuit
summary: Axon Enterprise Inc., the taser company, has agreed to disclose its corporate political spending for the next five years, settling a shareholder lawsuit. This agreement mandates public disclosure of contributions to politicians, parties, ballot initiatives, PACs, and political advertisements. The settlement resolves a lawsuit filed by the Nathan Cummings Foundation Inc., which had sought a ballot proposal on political spending.

title: Reassessing First Solar (FSLR) Valuation After Downgrades And Soft 2026 Guidance
summary: Recent analyst downgrades have prompted a reassessment of First Solar (FSLR) due to softer 2026 revenue guidance, a shrinking order backlog, and dependence on Section 45X tax credits, despite a positive 5-year return. While one user narrative suggests the stock is 25.3% overvalued with a fair value of $155.98, another analysis using multiples indicates it is undervalued by 27.7%. Investors are urged to examine key risks and opportunities to form their own conclusions.

title: Vertex Pharma to Seek Speedy FDA Approval for Kidney Disease Drug From $4.9B Acquisition
summary: Vertex Pharmaceuticals is seeking accelerated FDA approval for its immunology drug, povetacicept, for the treatment of immunoglobulin A nephropathy (IgAN). The drug, acquired from Alpine Immune Sciences, demonstrated a 52% reduction in urine protein levels in a Phase 3 study. Analysts believe povetacicept has best-in-class potential due to its efficacy, safety profile, and convenient monthly home administration via an autoinjector, despite competing with other similar drugs already on the market or in review.

title: Jim Cramer Discussed 8 Stocks Amid the S&P 500 Reshuffle: Vertiv, Paycom, and More
summary: Jim Cramer discussed the upcoming S&P 500 reshuffle, noting an increased emphasis on tech stocks, particularly those related to data centers. He highlighted that three out of four new entrants are data center plays, while one removed stock is an "AI exile" due to AI's impact. Cramer also specifically mentioned Paycom Software and Lamb Weston Holdings, discussing AI's disruptional threat to Paycom and the decline in Lamb Weston's stock price, possibly due to GLP-1 drugs or restaurant industry struggles.

title: Waste Management Inc Stock (WM) Moved Down by 4.53% on Mar 10: What Signal Does It Send?
summary: Waste Management Inc. (WM) stock declined by 4.53% due to a combination of profit-taking, insider selling, and evolving regulatory landscapes, alongside broader macroeconomic concerns. The stock was previously considered overvalued, and upcoming regulations requiring significant investment in the waste management sector are influencing investor sentiment. Additionally, rising oil prices and a potentially weakening labor market contribute to overall market uncertainty, despite the company's strong financial outlook.

title: Qualcomm faces loss of Apple business, competitive pressures, says Bank of America
summary: Bank of America initiated coverage on Qualcomm with an 'Underperform' rating and a $145 price target, citing expected modest long-term growth and significant competitive pressures. Analysts anticipate a substantial loss of Apple's modem business by 2027 as Apple shifts to internal chip development. While Qualcomm has growth opportunities in automotive and IoT, strong competition across all its markets remains a concern.

title: Amgen further shrinking Montgomery County footprint, cutting 22 positions
summary: Biopharmaceutical company Amgen is reducing its presence in Montgomery County, Maryland, by cutting 22 positions at its Rockville research site and giving up space at 9605 Medical Center Drive. This move follows previous downsizing efforts by the company in the region.

title: Is Old Dominion Freight Line Stock Outperforming the Dow?
summary: Old Dominion Freight Line, Inc. (ODFL) is a large-cap less-than-truckload motor carrier that has outperformed the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJI) over the past three months and year-to-date, despite a recent revenue decline. The company's stock is trading above its 50-day and 200-day moving averages, but analysts maintain a "Hold" rating, suggesting limited upside from its current price. While ODFL has shown strong short-term performance, it has lagged behind its competitor XPO, Inc. (XPO) over the past year.

title: PayPal Valuation Disconnect Widens Despite $6.4 Billion Free Cash Flow
summary: PayPal (PYPL) is trading at a significant discount, with a valuation implying structural decline despite robust financials including $6.4 billion in annual free cash flow and a massive share repurchase program. The article argues that the market is irrationally pricing the stock based on anxieties like branded checkout deceleration and CEO turnover, while ignoring strong performance in other segments like Venmo and a leadership team geared towards operational rigor. It concludes that the stock offers substantial upside given its undervaluation compared to its fundamental strength and ongoing capital returns.

title: DBV: Peanut allergy patch therapy nears launch with strong data, targeting young children and future pipeline growth
summary: DBV Technologies is moving forward with its epicutaneous immunotherapy for peanut allergy in young children, planning two Biologics License Application (BLA) submissions in 2026. The company has strong clinical data supporting its treatment and is also preparing for launch while expanding its pipeline to include cow's milk protein allergy. DBV Technologies is financially stable, with a robust cash position to support these initiatives.

title: Goldman Sachs (GS) Analyst Rating Update: JP Morgan Raises Price Target | GS Stock News
summary: JP Morgan has maintained a "Neutral" rating for Goldman Sachs (GS) but increased its price target from $815.00 to $826.00, a 1.35% rise. This update follows several other analysts in early 2026 who also raised their price targets for GS. Wall Street analysts predict an average target price of $962.28 for Goldman Sachs, implying a 15.01% upside from its current price, while GuruFocus estimates a potential downside of 17.09% based on its GF Value.

title: The Davis Companies closes on disposition of two self-storage facilities operated by Extra Space Storage
summary: The Davis Companies (Davis) have completed the sale of two self-storage facilities in Boston and Medford, MA, both operated by Extra Space Storage, to a joint venture between Invesco Real Estate and Baranof Holdings. Davis made significant capital improvements to both properties, increasing unit counts and overall facility performance, leading to strong occupancy rates. The sale highlights Davis's strategy of identifying opportunities, executing improvements, and delivering high returns, with the properties located in urban submarkets experiencing high demand and limited supply.

title: Market Whales and Their Recent Bets on UAL Options
summary: Large investors have been making significant options trades on United Airlines Holdings (UAL), with a split sentiment between bullish and bearish. These "whale" trades suggest anticipated price movements for UAL between $40.0 and $130.0 in the coming months. The article also notes UAL's current market standing, recent analyst ratings, and the potential risks and rewards of options trading.

title: Insider Sell: Kelly Rose Sells 8,500 Shares of ConocoPhillips (COP)
summary: Kelly Rose, Senior Vice President & General Counsel at ConocoPhillips, sold 8,500 shares of the company on March 9, 2026. After this transaction, Rose now owns 32,984 shares of ConocoPhillips. ConocoPhillips (COP) is a global energy company involved in the exploration, production, transport, and marketing of various energy resources.

title: Devon Energy: Not Priced For Oil Rally (NYSE:DVN)
summary: Devon Energy's stock has not fully reflected the recent surge in oil prices, despite geopolitical tensions. The company is actively pursuing a merger with Coterra Energy and plans dividend hikes and stock buybacks. With an 11.5% free cash flow yield based on future lower oil prices, the stock appears cheap, though market dynamics could introduce short-term volatility.

title: AbbVie’s GLP-1 challenger posts strong weight-loss results in early trial
summary: AbbVie's GLP-1 challenger drug has shown promising weight-loss results in its early trial stages. The article mentions this significant development by the pharmaceutical company, headquartered in Waukegan. Further details on the trial results are anticipated.

title: Data center colo results Q4 2025: Digital Realty, Equinix, Iron Mountain, American Tower
summary: The major publicly-listed data center colocation providers, including Digital Realty, Equinix, Iron Mountain, and American Tower, reported record revenues for 2025 and projected healthy growth for 2026. Digital Realty posted record financial performance with significant leasing and development. Equinix achieved record bookings and capacity delivery, expanding its global footprint. Iron Mountain saw substantial year-on-year growth in data center revenues and secured numerous new leases, while American Tower also reported continued data center revenue growth.

title: Gray Media Forces Blackout of 226 Local Channels, Disrupting DISH TV Customers Nationwide
summary: Gray Media has blacked out 226 local channels across 113 markets for DISH TV customers after DISH refused to accept what it called "unreasonable rate increases." DISH states that Gray Media is demanding high "retransmission consent" fees unrelated to declining viewership and is using viewers as "bargaining chips." DISH is urging customers to use over-the-air antennas or streaming apps to access local programming and remains ready to negotiate a fair deal.

title: Chinese telecom company fined $50M for conspiring to steal technology from Motorola Solutions
summary: A federal judge in Chicago has fined Hytera Communications Corp. LTD, a Chinese telecommunications company, $50 million for conspiring to steal proprietary digital mobile radio technology from Motorola Solutions. Hytera recruited former Motorola employees to steal trade secrets, including source code, to develop competing products at a significantly lower cost. The company pleaded guilty to conspiracy to steal trade secrets and was also ordered to pay approximately $214 million in restitution, offset by previous civil judgment payments.

title: New class action accuses Adobe of paying women less, then retaliating
summary: Adobe is facing a class action lawsuit filed by former employee Anna Buntjer, alleging that the company and staffing firm Talentburst systematically paid women less than men for equal work and retaliated against her after she reported sexual harassment and pay disparity. The lawsuit seeks to represent over 500 current and former female employees, claiming aggregate damages exceeding $5 million. The case also raises questions about joint employer responsibilities for equal pay and safe workplaces when staffing firms are involved.

title: Harvard Bioscience promotes Frost to CFO
summary: Mark Frost has been promoted to Chief Financial Officer and treasurer at Harvard Bioscience after serving as interim CFO for 11 months. His appointment follows challenges for the Holliston-based biotech firm, including regaining compliance with Nasdaq requirements regarding stock price and audit committee composition. Frost will receive an annual base salary of $375,000, with potential incentive compensation.

title: From Private to Public – the Importance of Reward and Remuneration
summary: This article discusses the critical steps companies must take to recalibrate their executive reward frameworks when transitioning from private to public ownership, particularly in the lead-up to an IPO. It emphasizes the need to align incentives with long-term value creation, reinforce shareholder alignment, and prepare for increased scrutiny from investors and regulators. The piece outlines five key considerations for updating remuneration strategies, including reviewing legacy arrangements, focusing on pay for performance, choosing appropriate metrics, onboarding a remuneration committee, and building effective internal and external communication strategies.

title: Relacorilant safe, works to control high blood pressure in Cushing’s: Study
summary: A Phase 3 clinical study (GRACE) published in The Lancet Diabetes & Endocrinology found that relacorilant, an experimental oral treatment by Corcept Therapeutics, is safe and effective for managing high blood pressure caused by excess cortisol in adults with endogenous Cushing’s syndrome. Despite the positive full data confirming hypertension control, the U.S. FDA previously declined to approve relacorilant, citing a need for clearer evidence that benefits outweigh risks, prompting Corcept to seek further guidance from the agency for potential reapplication. The study found common side effects included back pain and headache, with no cases of adrenal insufficiency or dangerous potassium lowering.

title: Do Mangrove Credits Reflect Real Biomass Growth?
summary: Mangrove carbon projects often have substantial belowground carbon stocks that are difficult to measure. The findings emphasize the importance of independent geospatial validation to ensure carbon credit issuances align with ecological outcomes and durable climate impact.

(NASDAQ: PYPL) common stock during the Class Period of February 25, 2025, to February 2, 2026, about the lead plaintiff deadline of April 20, 2026, in a securities fraud lawsuit. The lawsuit alleges that PayPal made materially false and misleading statements regarding its financial targets and growth potential, particularly concerning its salesforce's ability to execute these goals. Investors who purchased PayPal stock during this period may be entitled to compensation and are encouraged to contact the law firm to join the class action.

title: Paycom Software (NYSE:PAYC) Shares Down 4.8% - Time to Sell?
summary: Paycom Software (NYSE:PAYC) shares dropped 4.8% on Tuesday on unusually low trading volume. The stock is currently trading below its 50-day and 200-day moving averages, and analysts have recently cut price targets, resulting in a consensus "Hold" rating. Despite the downturn, the company reported a slight EPS beat and 10.2% YoY revenue growth, maintains profitability, and announced a quarterly dividend.

title: News Corporation Announces On-Market Buy-Back of Class A Securities to Enhance Shareholder Value
summary: News Corporation has announced an ongoing stock buyback program of up to $1 billion for its Class A and Class B common shares, initiated on July 15, 2025. This program aims to enhance shareholder value by reducing outstanding shares, potentially increasing earnings per share, and supporting the stock price. The buyback is conducted on the open market for cash, with daily disclosures to the ASX and updates in quarterly and annual filings.

title: Paul Walter Hauser Eyed for Role in Netflix’s Live-Action ‘Scooby-Doo’ Series
summary: Actor Paul Walter Hauser is reportedly being considered for the role of Scooby-Doo's original owner in Netflix's upcoming live-action "Scooby-Doo" series. This casting decision suggests a deeper exploration of the franchise's lore, potentially rewriting Scooby's traditional origin story with Shaggy. The series, expected to be titled "Scooby Doo: Origins," is set to begin filming in April in Atlanta and will explore how Mystery Inc. first formed.

title: Morgan Stanley Expects One Of The 'Most Expensive' Cybersecurity Stocks To Outperform Long Term
summary: Morgan Stanley has upgraded CrowdStrike (NASDAQ:CRWD) to "Overweight" with an increased price target, adding it to their Top Pick list. The firm believes CrowdStrike is well-positioned in the cybersecurity market due to its leadership in endpoint security, AI advantages, expanding product suite, and strong financial profile, justifying its premium valuation despite being one of the most expensive stocks in the sector. They expect the company to achieve over 20% revenue growth and more than 30% free cash flow growth in the next three years.

title: Lockheed Martin's Sikorsky, Robinson Launch Autonomous Cargo Helicopter
summary: Lockheed Martin's Sikorsky and Robinson Helicopter Company have unveiled the R66 Turbinetruck, an autonomous cargo helicopter designed for military resupply and civil logistics. The aircraft integrates Robinson Unmanned's cargo UAS airframe with Sikorsky's Matrix autonomy system, which has over 1,000 flight hours across various platforms. This development comes as Lockheed Martin's stock has seen significant growth, partly due to increased defense spending and geopolitical tensions.

title: Coca-Cola Stock Is Crushing the Market This Year. Is It Time to Buy?
summary: Coca-Cola's stock has significantly outperformed the S&P 500 this year, driven by strong fourth-quarter and full-year 2025 earnings and robust organic revenue growth. Despite a premium valuation, the company's steady organic growth, robust cash flow, and reliable dividend make it an attractive option for investors seeking stability and income. While the stock's high price-to-earnings ratio demands careful consideration, the underlying business strength and dividend yield arguably justify its current price.

title: Grid Dynamics, Gartner, Maximus, Insperity, and TaskUs Shares Are Falling, What You Need To Know
summary: Several stocks, including Grid Dynamics, Gartner, Maximus, Insperity, and TaskUs, experienced afternoon declines due to escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East and a "risk-off" sentiment among investors. Despite the U.S. President's comments suggesting the conflict was nearing an end, the market reacted negatively, leading to significant price drops across these companies. The article specifically highlights Insperity's volatility, noting its recent drop following news of new global tariffs and its overall poor performance year-to-date.

title: Kimberly-Clark appoints digital-focused executive
summary: Kimberly-Clark has appointed Francesco Tinto as chief information and global business services officer, a move that reinforces the company's commitment to digital operations and shared services. Tinto brings extensive experience in enterprise technology and digital transformation from previous roles at Advantage Solutions, Walgreens Boots Alliance, and Kraft Heinz. His appointment is expected to strengthen Kimberly-Clark's "Powering Care strategy" by combining technology leadership with operational expertise.

title: General Motors Co Stock (GM) Moved Up by 3.17% on Mar 10: Facts Behind the Movement
summary: General Motors (GM) stock rose by 3.17% on March 10, driven by a share repurchase program, increased dividends, improved analyst ratings, and better-than-expected Q4 2025 adjusted EPS. Despite a net loss due to EV-related charges and some intraday volatility, the company's strategic product launches and potential benefits from higher oil prices contributed to positive sentiment. However, technical indicators suggest caution, and the broader automotive industry faces a negative earnings outlook.

title: Form 424B2 GOLDMAN SACHS GROUP INC
summary: This SEC filing (Form 424B2) from Goldman Sachs Group Inc. details an offering of SPDR Gold Trust-Linked Notes. These notes do not bear interest, and their maturity payment is based on the performance of the SPDR Gold Trust (ETF), subject to a maximum settlement amount of $1,132.2 and a minimum of $950 per $1,000 face amount. The document outlines the terms, hypothetical examples of returns, and significant risk factors, including credit risk, market volatility, and tax implications, noting that the notes are treated as contingent payment debt instruments for U.S. federal income tax purposes.

title: Corning Inc (GLW) Stock Price Up 7.43% on Mar 10
summary: Corning Inc (GLW) saw its stock price climb by 7.43% on March 10, reaching an intraday high of $138.99 before closing at $138.64. This places the stock 14.47% below its 52-week high and significantly above its 52-week low. Wall Street analysts have an average target price of $127.54, implying an 8.01% downside, with a consensus "Outperform" rating, while GuruFocus estimates a GF Value of $61.27, suggesting a 55.81% downside.

title: Form 424B2 GOLDMAN SACHS GROUP INC
summary: GS Finance Corp. is offering Autocallable Buffered Basket-Linked Notes due March 16, 2028, guaranteed by The Goldman Sachs Group, Inc. These notes do not bear interest, with returns based on the performance of an equally weighted basket of common stocks from Bank of America, Capital One, Morgan Stanley, and Wells Fargo. The notes feature an automatic call if the basket level meets or exceeds the initial level on March 29, 2027, resulting in a payment of at least $1,199.5 per $1,000 face amount, and include a buffer level to mitigate losses at maturity.

title: Fair Isaac (NYSE:FICO) Stock Price Down 5.9% - What's Next?
summary: Fair Isaac (NYSE:FICO) stock dropped 5.9% on Tuesday to $1,355.70 on light trading volume. Despite the stock decline, the company surpassed quarterly expectations with $7.33 EPS against an estimated $7.08, reported revenue of $766 million (+16.4% y/y), and authorized a $1.5 billion share buyback program. Analyst sentiment remains largely positive, with a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating and a mean target price of $1,994.62.

title: SAIC, Verisk, Magnite, CoStar, and Fair Isaac Corporation Shares Are Falling, What You Need To Know
summary: Shares of SAIC, Verisk, Magnite, CoStar, and Fair Isaac Corporation dropped amidst escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, leading to a "risk-off" sentiment among investors despite U.S. presidential comments suggesting the conflict was near an end. The market's reaction highlights how investors prioritize immediate military actions over political assurances. The article notes that significant price drops can create buying opportunities for high-quality stocks and details Fair Isaac Corporation's recent volatility and year-to-date performance.

title: Cogent, ADP, Barrett, Brink's, and Equifax Stocks Trade Down, What You Need To Know
summary: A number of stocks, including Cogent (CCOI), ADP (ADP), Barrett (BBSI), Brink's (BCO), and Equifax (EFX), experienced a downturn after reports of escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East led to a "risk-off" sentiment among investors. The market reacted negatively despite political assurances, focusing instead on immediate military actions. The article highlights ADP's recent volatility, noting its significant drop due to renewed trade policy uncertainty previously and its overall year-to-date decline.

title: EPAM Systems Inc (EPAM) Stock Price Down 3.02% on Mar 10
summary: EPAM Systems Inc (EPAM) stock fell by 3.02% in mid-day trading on March 10, reaching an intraday low of $138.62 before closing at $141.85. Wall Street analysts have an average one-year price target of $192.10 for EPAM, indicating a potential upside of 35.42%, with a consensus "Outperform" recommendation. GuruFocus estimates the fair value (GF Value) for EPAM at $283.49, suggesting a nearly 100% upside from its current price.

title: How Conan O’Brien Is Getting Ready for the 2026 Oscars® on ABC and Hulu
summary: Conan O’Brien is preparing to host the 2026 Oscars® on ABC and Hulu, marking his return after a successful 2025 telecast that achieved high ratings. O'Brien, who enjoyed his previous hosting experience, joked about changing his pre-show meal from a heavy Italian supper to a light Japanese bento box. He and his writing team are also diligently crafting jokes for the ceremony, scheduled for March 15th at the Dolby Theatre, expressing that getting a laugh remains as exciting as ever.

title: BTIG Raises Costco (COST) Target as Membership Income and Traffic Stay Strong
summary: BTIG analyst Bob Drbul raised Costco's price target to $1,125 from $1,115, maintaining a Buy rating after the company's Q2 earnings beat. Strong membership income growth, increased gross margin, healthy traffic, and digital performance were cited as key factors. Costco also reported strategies to combat tariffs, price reductions on certain goods, and plans for significant warehouse expansion.

title: ConocoPhillips Is Shifting From Growth Mode to Cash Harvesting at a Critical Moment
summary: ConocoPhillips (COP) is transitioning from a growth-oriented strategy to a cash harvesting model after acquiring Marathon Oil (MRO). Despite a 26% year-to-date increase in its stock and doubled synergy targets from the acquisition, the company faces headwinds due to falling oil prices, which led to a 37.3% drop in Q4 net income. Analysts project significant cash flow growth through 2030, but the success of the Marathon integration relies on Brent crude averaging around $70 per barrel.

title: Cboe Global Markets (NASDAQ:CBOE) Stock Price Down 4.8% - Should You Sell?
summary: Cboe Global Markets (NASDAQ:CBOE) saw its stock price drop 4.8% in mid-day trading to $284.88, with trading volume significantly below average. This decline occurred despite the company reporting a quarterly earnings beat and strong revenue growth. Analysts have a mixed view on the stock, with a "Hold" consensus rating, while recent insider transactions show selling activity.

title: UBS Boosts Bristol-Myers Squibb (BMY) Price Target as Milvexian Data Looms
summary: UBS has increased its price target for Bristol-Myers Squibb (BMY) to $70 from $65, maintaining a Buy rating, ahead of anticipated Phase III data for milvexian. Additionally, Bristol-Myers Squibb announced positive late-stage trial results for its experimental blood cancer drug, mezigdomide, which improved survival in patients with relapsed multiple myeloma. The company's research and development efforts are focused on various serious diseases, including oncology and cardiovascular conditions.

title: How Citigroup Plans to Achieve 5-6% Y/Y NII Growth in 2026?
summary: Citigroup expects its net interest income (NII) excluding markets to grow 5-6% year-over-year in 2026, building on a 6% growth in 2025. This growth is anticipated to be driven by business mix, loan growth in Cards and Wealth, and strong reinvestment income as fixed-rate securities mature into higher-yielding instruments. While declining interest rates are expected to partially offset NII growth, Citigroup's strategy focuses on expanding lending, growing deposits, and reinvesting at higher yields to achieve its targets, aligning with peers like Bank of America and JPMorgan.

title: Is it Time to Sell Blackstone Stock Amid Rising Private Credit Woes?
summary: Shares of Blackstone (BX) have declined significantly over the past three months due to concerns about the private credit market, fund outflows, and macroeconomic pressures. While the company faces immediate challenges from investor sentiment and potential redemptions, its strong AUM, diversified product base, and effective fundraising position it well for long-term growth. Investors should monitor evolving private credit risks before making new investment decisions, though current holders might consider retaining their shares.

title: Form 424B2 CITIGROUP INC
summary: Citigroup Global Markets Holdings Inc., guaranteed by Citigroup Inc., has filed a Form 424B2 pricing supplement for Autocallable Contingent Coupon Equity Linked Securities due September 10, 2027. These securities are linked to the performance of the Nasdaq-100 Index®, Russell 2000® Index, and S&P 500® Index, offering periodic contingent coupon payments that require the worst-performing underlying to be above its coupon barrier. The securities carry significant risks, including potential loss of principal, limited liquidity, and sensitivity to market volatility of the underlying indices.

title: Wall Street Unveils AI Winners: Arista And Ciena Lead A Multi-Billion Dollar Backbone Explosion
summary: Needham analyst Ryan Koontz identified several networking and optical technology companies poised to benefit from the increasing hyperscale cloud spending driven by AI infrastructure expansion. Companies like Arista Networks and Ciena are leading in AI network expansion, while optical component makers such as Lumentum, Applied Optoelectronics, and Coherent are seeing strong demand. Additionally, manufacturing and testing suppliers like Fabrinet and Viavi Solutions are benefiting from the AI buildout, positioning these companies for long-term growth in cloud and AI.

title: J.P. Morgan Maintains Aptiv PLC(APTV.US) With Buy Rating, Maintains Target Price $105
summary: J.P. Morgan analyst Ryan Brinkman has reiterated a "buy" rating for Aptiv PLC (APTV.US) and maintained a target price of $105. According to TipRanks data, this analyst has a 50.5% success rate and an average return of -1.1% over the past year. This report highlights the analyst's stance on Aptiv, based on independent third-party analysis.

title: RBC Capital Markets lifts Broadcom (AVGO) price target, points to bullish FY27 commentary
summary: RBC Capital Markets has increased its price target for Broadcom (AVGO) to $1,500, maintaining an Outperform rating due to a bullish outlook for its AI and software revenues. The firm's analysis, following discussions with Broadcom's CFO, highlights strong growth expectations, particularly in AI, and stable margins, with potential for further upside from acquisitions and capital returns.

title: Data center colo results Q4 2025: Digital Realty, Equinix, Iron Mountain, American Tower
summary: Major publicly-listed data center colocation providers, including Digital Realty, Equinix, Iron Mountain, and American Tower, reported record revenues for 2025 and projected strong growth for 2026. Each company detailed significant financial performance, new lease agreements, capacity expansions, and strategic investments, particularly in response to increasing demand from cloud and AI sectors. The reports indicate a robust and expanding data center market.

title: Adobe Stock Drops As Analyst Cuts Target On Growth Concerns
summary: Adobe's stock is declining after TD Cowen analyst Derrick Wood lowered the price target from $400 to $325, citing concerns about uneven growth trends heading into earnings. This reduction is based on weaker third-party credit-card data and a partner survey indicating a reduced 2026 growth outlook. Despite some bearish sentiment, TD Cowen believes Wall Street's net-new ARR estimates might be overly pessimistic, suggesting potential for Adobe to outperform.

title: Optical-Tech Stocks Looking Sharp: Ciena Up 9%, Lumentum Rises 8%, Coherent Climbs 7%
summary: Optical technology stocks, including Ciena, Lumentum, and Coherent, experienced significant gains driven by increasing demand from AI infrastructure buildouts. Ciena's strong Q1 revenue report, Lumentum and Coherent's addition to the S&P 500, and NVIDIA's investments in these companies are key catalysts. The surge highlights the growing importance of optical networking equipment as bandwidth becomes a bottleneck for AI scaling.

title: Chicken wings are in more than twice as many Casey’s stores versus a year ago
summary: Casey's has significantly expanded its chicken wing offering, now available in over 550 stores, more than doubling its presence from 225 stores a year ago. The company’s CEO, Darren Rebelez, noted that the chicken wings have proven largely incremental, complementing pizza sales, and the company reported a 49.3% increase in net income for the third quarter. Casey's also achieved strong financial performance with increased inside and fuel gross profit, driving an expected 18% to 20% increase in fiscal 2026 EBITDA.

title: Medtronic to strengthen neurovascular business with $550m Scientia Vascular acquisition
summary: Medtronic has agreed to acquire Scientia Vascular for $550 million, plus potential earn-out and milestone payments, to expand its neurovascular guidewire market presence. This acquisition will add Scientia Vascular's portfolio of access microcatheters and micro guidewires, aiming to enhance Medtronic's offerings for neurovascular conditions like acute ischemic stroke. GlobalData analysis suggests that Medtronic could significantly grow Scientia's market share globally by leveraging its international presence, potentially increasing sales to $80m-$100m.

title: IBM (IBM): Buy, Sell, or Hold Post Q4 Earnings?
summary: This article analyzes IBM's post-Q4 earnings performance, classifying it as a "Hold" rather than a "Buy" or "Sell." The analysis points to disappointing long-term revenue growth (4.1% CAGR over five years) and modest EPS growth (5.9% annually), leading to a cautious outlook despite the company trading at 20.8x forward P/E. The authors suggest looking for alternative investment opportunities in more exciting sectors.

title: Union Square Park Sells Mohawk Industries Stock
summary: Union Square Park Capital Management, LLC has sold its entire 40,000-share position in Mohawk Industries during the fourth quarter, as disclosed in a recent SEC filing. This divestment comes as Mohawk Industries' shares have struggled in a weak housing market, leading Union Square to seek potentially better return prospects elsewhere. Despite the sale, Mohawk remains a leading flooring brand, although its performance is tied to the cyclical nature of the housing industry.

title: Southern Company (SO) Price Target Raised by TD Cowen on Financing and Demand Outlook
summary: TD Cowen has raised its price target for Southern Company (SO) to $112 from $108, maintaining a Buy rating. This adjustment follows a significant $26.54 billion loan from the U.S. Department of Energy, which the firm views as both a financing advantage and political endorsement of SO's growth strategy. The analyst suggests that large increases in electricity demand can benefit customers and help cover system costs, potentially leading to a higher valuation premium for the company.

title: Pangaea Logistics Solutions Ltd. Reports Financial Results for the Fourth Quarter Ended December 31, 2025
summary: Pangaea Logistics Solutions Ltd. announced its financial results for the fourth quarter ended December 31, 2025, reporting GAAP net income of $11.9 million and adjusted EBITDA of $28.7 million. The company achieved Time Charter Equivalent (TCE) rates of $17,773 per day, exceeding Baltic indices by 19%, and declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.05 per common share. Pangaea highlighted strong Arctic trade activity, robust utilization of its ice-class fleet, and strategic investments in terminal operations as key drivers for its performance and future growth.

title: Louisiana nears deal with ConocoPhillips over coastal erosion
summary: Louisiana is close to finalizing a settlement with ConocoPhillips regarding litigation over the oil major's role in the state's coastal erosion. Governor Jeff Landry announced the tentative agreement, stressing the need for coastal parishes' leadership to conclude the process. This deal addresses lawsuits initiated by several parishes in 2013, which accused oil companies of failing to secure proper permits for drilling activities and sought damages for coastal degradation.

title: General Mills taps interim CSCO for top supply chain post
summary: General Mills has appointed Jonathan Ness as its permanent Chief Supply Chain Officer (CSCO), effective March 16, 2026. Ness, a company veteran with nearly 20 years of experience, has been serving as interim CSCO since late January. He will report directly to CEO Jeff Harmening and oversee global supply chain operations, succeeding Paul Gallagher who moved to 3M.

title: PNC sees retail and office real estate opportunities after years of underinvestment
summary: PNC is identifying new lending opportunities in commercial real estate sectors, specifically retail and office properties in strong markets like New York. This shift comes after years of primarily concentrating on multifamily housing, signaling a renewed interest in areas that have experienced underinvestment.

title: 10 Information Technology Stocks Whale Activity In Today's Session
summary: This article identifies significant options activity, referred to as "whale activity," across ten information technology stocks, including NVIDIA, Microsoft, and AMD. It details specific bullish, bearish, or neutral option trades, including contract types, expiration dates, strike prices, and total trade values, to help traders recognize potential market opportunities. The piece also provides a brief explanation of options terminology for clarity.

title: Avoiding the New Form 1099-DA Headaches: Six Essentials for Smooth Digital Asset Reporting
summary: With the introduction of Form 1099-DA for digital asset transactions starting in 2025, cryptocurrency brokers will report gross proceeds directly to the IRS and taxpayers. This article outlines six essential considerations including understanding that the form only reports proceeds, not the full taxable picture, and the need for taxpayers and preparers to reconcile these forms with comprehensive transaction histories from all platforms and wallets. It emphasizes that many digital asset events beyond "cash-out" are taxable, and income-generating activities still require separate reporting.

title: Futu Holdings jumps 7.6% as traders position ahead of earnings and China tech sentiment improves
summary: Futu Holdings (FUTU) saw its stock price jump 7.6% ahead of its Q4 and full-year 2025 earnings release, scheduled for March 12, 2026. This increase is attributed to pre-earnings positioning, improved sentiment in China/Hong Kong tech markets, and significant options activity indicating bullish views. Analysts have issued "Buy" and "Overweight" ratings for FUTU, with a median price target of $218.5.

title: Texas Instruments (TXN) Signals Recovery in Factory and Automation Markets, CEO Says
summary: Texas Instruments CEO Haviv Ilan announced a recovery in the company's industrial business, noting a broad return of demand after a period concentrated in specific sectors. During a recent conference, Ilan reported nearly 20% year-over-year industrial revenue growth in the fourth quarter, with traditional industrial markets like factory automation, medical equipment, and building automation showing renewed strength. This rebound is seen as a return to long-term trends after a significant downturn in these segments.

title: 3M is helping empower the next generation of manufacturing leaders
summary: 3M is investing in the next generation of manufacturing leaders through its Manufacturing & Academic Partnerships (MAP) program, which provides schools with mechatronics equipment, scholarships, and faculty training. The company is also transforming learning environments into "Future Makers Lab by 3M" spaces. Since 2017, the MAP program has supported over 5,000 students in communities where 3M operates, with plans for continued expansion and deeper community partnerships in 2026.

title: Duke Energy deal promises hundreds of millions for Carolinas customers
summary: Duke Energy has reached agreements with various North Carolina stakeholders, including customer advocates and the Attorney General's office, for the proposed combination of Duke Energy Carolinas and Duke Energy Progress. This settlement guarantees hundreds of millions in future customer savings over a 14-year period, with independent analysis projecting approximately $2.3 billion in savings from 2027 to 2040. The agreement emphasizes operational and capital-cost efficiencies, such as reduced fuel use and the elimination of 200 MW of planned battery storage while maintaining reliability.

title: Ford announces Super Duty models at Work Truck Week
summary: Ford Motor Co. announced new features for its 2027 Super Duty models at Work Truck Week in Indianapolis. Orders for these models will open in spring 2026. Key enhancements include expanding the Tremor off-road package to all models and adding Platinum trim options for chassis cabs.

title: Kentucky American Water Announces Nearly $72 Million Invested in Infrastructure Upgrades in 2025
summary: Kentucky American Water invested nearly $72 million in 2025 for water and wastewater system upgrades across its Kentucky service areas. These investments aim to strengthen system reliability, modernize aging infrastructure, and improve water quality for customers. Highlights include improvements at the Kentucky River Station I Water Treatment Plant, replacement of over 12 miles of water mains, and upgrades to hydrants, meters, and valves.

title: Amazon Trades at a Premium P/E: Time to Hold or Fold the Stock?
summary: Amazon (AMZN) completed 2025 with strong operational results, including record revenues and accelerated cloud growth, yet its stock has not gained momentum. The company's significant capital investments, particularly a projected $200 billion in 2026 for AWS AI infrastructure, are pressuring near-term cash flows and contributing to an overvalued stock from a valuation perspective, trading at a 12-month forward P/E of 26.46X compared to the industry's 21.77X. Despite these near-term concerns, the article suggests that existing investors should hold AMZN shares due to its long-term growth potential and diversified revenue, while new investors might consider waiting for a more favorable entry point.

title: Coca-Cola Stock Is Crushing the Market This Year. Is It Time to Buy?
summary: Coca-Cola's stock has significantly outperformed the S&P 500 this year, driven by strong organic revenue growth in 2025 and projections for continued growth in 2026. While the stock trades at a premium valuation with a price-to-earnings ratio of 26, its robust free cash flow, 2.7% dividend yield, and predictable business model suggest it remains a solid buy for investors seeking stability and dividend income, despite potential valuation compression risks.

title: Scotiabank Lifts Verizon (VZ) to Outperform, Calls Shares “Cheap” at Current Levels
summary: Scotiabank has upgraded Verizon (VZ) to Outperform, raising its price target to $54.50, citing strong subscriber additions, cost improvements, and "cheap" share prices. This upgrade follows similar positive sentiments from Wolfe Research and Daiwa Capital Markets, which highlight Verizon's steady profits, consistent dividend increases, and stable business model as appealing to investors in uncertain economic times. Verizon's long track record of dividend payments and focus on increasing them further strengthen its appeal as a reliable telecom investment.

title: Xylem brings four instruments to Pittcon 2026, led by MultiLab Pro IDS multi-parameter platform
summary: Xylem Lab Solutions unveiled four new instruments at Pittcon 2026, emphasizing their ability to help labs achieve more with fewer resources. The leading product was the MultiLab Pro IDS multi-parameter benchtop meter, designed for enhanced compliance and usability with its intelligent digital sensor platform. Xylem also showcased a combustion analyzer for nitrogen/protein determination, a biochemistry analyzer for blood glucose, and a selective VOC analyzer for drinking water.

title: American Electric Power (AEP) Gets Price Target Hikes from Evercore ISI and Argus
summary: Evercore ISI and Argus have both raised their price targets for American Electric Power (AEP). Evercore ISI increased its recommendation to $153 from $137 with an Outperform rating, while Argus lifted its target to $140 from $128, maintaining a Buy rating. This comes as AEP reaffirmed its 2026 operating earnings guidance and long-term growth outlook, supported by a significant capital plan for incremental demand and new projects.

title: Assessing Coca-Cola Consolidated (COKE) Valuation After Independence Deal And Strong Fundamental Performance
summary: Coca-Cola Consolidated (COKE) recently became fully independent after buying out Coca-Cola's equity interest for $2.4 billion, a move that coincides with strong stock performance and a 31.17% return over 30 days. Despite trading at $206.91, Simply Wall St's intrinsic value estimate suggests a 25% discount, with a popular narrative valuing it much higher at $1,566.98 due to its strong brand, distribution, and economies of scale. However, potential risks like increased costs from tighter sugar/packaging rules or supply chain issues could impact margins, necessitating a balanced look at the company's reward and warning signs.

title: Howmet Aerospace (HWM) Sets Bold 2026 Targets as Aerospace Sector Scales All-Time Highs
summary: Howmet Aerospace (HWM) unveiled aggressive growth targets at its Technology & Markets Day on March 10, 2026, amid record highs in the aerospace sector. The company projects $9.1 billion in revenue for 2026 and highlighted its critical role in military modernization and commercial aviation, with significant growth in its Engine Products segment. Howmet is also strategically pivoting into industrial gas turbines for data centers, aiming for continued margin expansion and free cash flow generation.

title: Baird Upgrades Union Pacific (UNP) and Raises Target, Sees Upside from Potential Rail Deal
summary: Baird has upgraded Union Pacific (UNP) to Outperform, raising its price target to $311 from $239, citing potential cost synergies from a rail deal with Norfolk Southern that could exceed initial estimates. The firm recommends accumulating shares during periods of weakness, anticipating improved sentiment by 2026. This follows a recent price target increase by BofA, which also maintained a Buy rating for UNP.

title: CRL: Biotech demand is recovering, tech investments continue, and acquisitions strengthen core focus
summary: Charles River Laboratories (CRL) is experiencing a rebound in demand due to improved biotech funding, evidenced by positive trends in bookings and backlog. The company is advancing its technological investments, particularly in AI, and refining its core business strategies through recent acquisitions and divestitures to enhance margins. China's market is noted for both its competitive pressures and significant growth opportunities for CRL.

title: Stryker (SYK) Expands Digital Healthcare Push with New SmartHospital Platform
summary: Stryker Corporation (NYSE: SYK) has launched its new SmartHospital Platform, aiming to integrate hospital devices, data, and care teams into a single ecosystem. Developed by Styker's Smart Care business, this platform seeks to address challenges like fragmented systems, staffing shortages, and increasing patient volumes through features like voice-activated tools, alarm prioritization, and the use of AI and ambient sensors. The initiative expands Stryker's digital healthcare offerings, enhancing patient care and workflow efficiency.

title: RBC Capital Markets Lifts Broadcom (AVGO) Price Target, Points to Bullish FY27 Commentary
summary: RBC Capital Markets raised its price target for Broadcom (AVGO) to $360 from $340, maintaining a Sector Perform rating, citing a solid Q2 outlook and bullish FY27 commentary. The firm highlighted Broadcom's consistent execution. Broadcom's CEO reported record Q1 2026 revenue of $19.3 billion, driven by AI semiconductors, and expects continued growth with consolidated revenue projected at $22 billion for Q2 2026.

title: Lead Real Estate Co., Ltd Announces Delivery of EXCELSIOR NAKAMEGURO in Tokyo
summary: Lead Real Estate Co., Ltd (LRE) announced the delivery of its income-producing condominium property, EXCELSIOR NAKAMEGURO, in Tokyo after executing a sales contract on February 17, 2026, and completing delivery on February 27, 2026. This 10-unit reinforced concrete condominium is located in Kamimeguro and demonstrates the company's successful development and disposition strategy, fulfilling ongoing demand for its Excelsior series. CEO Eiji Nagahara highlighted opportunities for further expansion of this product line in established residential areas of Tokyo.

title: Tattoo-Inspired QSR Promotions
summary: Chipotle Mexican Grill is bringing back its "Tatted Like a Chipotle Bag" promotion for Friday the 13th, offering a buy-one-get-one-free deal to customers with any tattoo. The campaign also features a partnership with musician Swae Lee to create exclusive temporary tattoos. This initiative aims to deepen the brand's connection with the tattoo community and leverage a popular internet meme into a real-world fan experience.

title: Can Clorox's Digital Push Accelerate Operational Efficiency?
summary: The Clorox Company is focusing on digital transformation, including the completion of its U.S. ERP implementation, to enhance operational efficiency, streamline decision-making, and improve execution. While the transition presented some short-term challenges, Clorox believes these investments will lead to stronger productivity, margin resilience, and long-term growth. The company's shares have gained 10.2% in the past three months and it currently holds a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold), with future earnings growth projected.

title: Capricor Therapeutics Sees Surge in Trading Volume
summary: Capricor Therapeutics (NASDAQ:CAPR) experienced a nearly 15% surge in its share price and a 95% increase in trading volume on Tuesday, signaling heightened investor interest in the company's cell and exosome-based therapies. Despite mixed ratings from research firms, analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus with a target price of $40.82. The increased attention is likely due to the progress of its lead candidate, CAP-1002, for Duchenne muscular dystrophy and COVID-19-related heart injury.

title: Vögele Super 20003-5 X From: Wirtgen Group, a John Deere Co.

Marion County has sought a court injunction to ensure cleanup continues, while a class-action lawsuit has also been filed against CSX and other railroad companies regarding the storage of the toxic ties.

title: Opening arguments begin in first Illinois trial against Abbott over its formula for premature babies

Abbott, however, contends the formula is not dangerous and that additional warnings would not have prevented the illnesses, arguing that the absence of human milk, rather than formula exposure, is linked to an increased risk of NEC.

This trial seeks to establish CyPath Lung as a standard of care for early-stage lung cancer assessment, leveraging its reported 92% sensitivity, 87% specificity, and 88% accuracy from previous trials.
