Baldry predicts continued revenue headwinds in the first half of 2026 but expects profitability to persist, indicating a stable outlook for the stock.

title: Herbalife (HLF) Debt Refi and Ronaldo Tie-Up: Signal of Strength or Masking Core Strains?
summary: Herbalife (HLF) recently reported strong Q4 2025 earnings, announced a $1.55 billion debt refinancing plan, and highlighted Cristiano Ronaldo's investment in Pro2col, a technology linked to the company. These positive developments occur despite ongoing challenges such as declining quarterly sales volumes and falling earnings per share. The article questions whether these recent events indicate true strength or merely mask underlying business pressures, with the refinancing potentially offering short-term financial flexibility but not necessarily resolving long-term demand and earnings issues.

title: AMTD Reached Deals on the Acquisition of a London Office Tower
summary: AMTD IDEA Group has agreed to acquire an office building in London for approximately US$24.0 million and secured a US$51.0 million loan to finance this and the previously announced acquisition of the London Hornsey Town Hall project. The total consideration for these transactions is about US$87.7 million, strengthening AMTD's presence in European hospitality and commercial real estate through a strategic partnership with FEC.

title: FTC Solar secures 1GW tracker supply deal with Strata
summary: FTC Solar, Inc. has announced a five-year extension to its tracker supply agreement with Strata Clean Energy, adding 1 gigawatt of solar tracker systems. This builds on a previous 500-megawatt agreement, with the first project under the new extension expected in the second half of 2027. Despite recent stock volatility and a net loss, the company reported significant revenue growth, and analysts consider the stock potentially undervalued.

title: FTC Solar rises after signing 1,000 MW tracker supply deal with Strata Clean Energy (FTCI:NASDAQ)
summary: FTC Solar (FTCI) saw a 10.2% rise in trading after announcing a five-year, 1 GW expansion to its tracker supply agreement with Strata Clean Energy. This new agreement is set to begin in the second half of 2027 and follows a previous three-year, 500 MW deal between the two companies.

title: Erasca secures worldwide rights to RAS-targeting drug ERAS-0015
summary: Erasca Inc. (NASDAQ:ERAS) has expanded its license agreement with Joyo Pharmatech Co., Ltd. to secure worldwide rights for ERAS-0015, an oral pan-RAS molecular glue targeting RAS-mutant cancers. The company's stock is trading near its 52-week high, reflecting substantial growth, and ERAS-0015 is currently undergoing Phase 1 trials with promising early results. Analysts have also raised price targets for Erasca following recent financial and strategic developments.

title: Immutep Partners with Flex Databases to Digitize Clinical Trial Documentation
summary: Immutep GmbH has partnered with Flex Databases to implement their eTMF solution, aiming to digitize and improve the management of clinical trial documentation. This transition from a paper-based system addresses previous issues of limited visibility and manual effort for audit readiness, enhancing efficiency, compliance, and collaboration. The move underscores Immutep’s commitment to modernizing its clinical trial operations with a focus on regulatory compliance and cost-effective solutions.

title: OCS: Late-stage pipeline targets DME, dry eye, and neuroprotection, with major data readouts in 2024
summary: Oculis Holding AG (OCS) is advancing three Phase III programs with major data readouts expected in 2024. These include a topical therapy for diabetic macular edema (DME), a biomarker-driven dry eye trial with Q4 results anticipated, and a neuroprotection candidate, Privosegtor, showing strong efficacy in optic neuritis. Premium pricing is expected for Privosegtor.

title: Southern Copper Corp Stock (SCCO) Moved Up by 3.73% on Mar 10: What Investors Need To Know
summary: Southern Copper Corporation (SCCO) saw its stock rise by 3.73% on March 10th, driven by a bullish outlook for copper prices, with demand fueled by electric vehicles, AI, and renewable energy. Despite some previous analyst concerns and insider share sales, the strong long-term demand fundamentals for copper appear to be outweighing other factors. The company shows strong operating performance and profitability, with analysts providing varied ratings but acknowledging its growth potential.

title: Shareholders who lost money in shares of Driven Brands
summary: Wolf Haldenstein Adler Freeman & Herz LLP has announced a securities class action lawsuit against Driven Brands Holdings Inc. (NASDAQ: DRVN) and its officers. The lawsuit alleges that the company made materially false or misleading statements and failed to disclose significant accounting errors between May 9, 2023, and February 24, 2026. Investors who suffered losses have until May 8, 2026, to seek appointment as lead plaintiff.

title: Best Momentum Stocks to Buy for March 10th
summary: This article identifies three top momentum stocks for March 10th: Sterling Infrastructure (STRL), Liquidia Corporation (LQDA), and NWPX Infrastructure (NWPX). All three companies hold a Zacks Rank #1 and have seen positive revisions to their current year earnings estimates. The article also highlights their strong stock performance relative to the S&P 500 over the past three months and their strong Momentum Scores.

title: Polyrizon Announces GMP Manufacturing of Clinical Trial Material (CTM) with Eurofins CDMO AmatsiAquitaine S.A.S for Planned U.S. Clinical Study
summary: Polyrizon Ltd. announced the successful Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) batch production of clinical trial material for its lead product candidate, in collaboration with Eurofins CDMO AmatsiAquitaine S.A.S. This critical step supports Polyrizon's preparations for a planned U.S. clinical study later this year and confirms the consistency and stability of their manufacturing process. The company's CEO, Tomer Izraeli, highlighted this milestone as a significant advancement in their clinical development strategy for innovative intranasal solutions against airborne allergens and viruses.

title: NPCE RNS System Explained: Growth Drivers and Setup for 2026
summary: NeuroPace, Inc. (NPCE) is experiencing significant growth with its RNS System, a brain-responsive implant for drug-resistant epilepsy, driven by strong sales in 2025 and expanding margins. New Medicare payment changes effective in 2026 are expected to further boost hospital and physician economics for replacement procedures. The company's focus on specialized epilepsy centers and an expanding commercial footprint are key to its revenue model, with an optimistic outlook for 2026 sales despite expected front-loaded spending.

title: Optical Cable 10-Q: Revenue $16.4M, Loss per Share $(0.05)
summary: Optical Cable (OCC) reported first-quarter net sales of $16.4 million, a 4.4% increase year-over-year, and a net loss of $(0.05) per share, improving from $(0.14) in the prior year. The company saw expanded gross margins and reduced operating losses, attributed to stronger volumes, operational leverage, and growing international demand. Strategic partnerships and growth in enterprise and specialty markets also contributed to the improved financial performance.

title: Innolux selling factory in Tainan to ChipMOS
summary: Innolux has announced the sale of a panel module assembly facility in Tainan to ChipMOS Technologies Inc. for NT$880 million (US$27.65 million). This move is part of Innolux's efforts to rejuvenate assets and improve profitability by optimizing its product portfolio. ChipMOS will use the facility to expand its capacity amidst growing demand for memory and logic chips in AI-related devices.

title: Profits spark at Kohl's despite slide in sales
summary: Kohl's reported a surge in net income in the fourth quarter and for the full year 2025, despite experiencing a slide in net sales during the crucial holiday period. The company attributed the profit increase to strong inventory management, reduced administrative costs, and a one-off gain from a legal settlement. CEO Michael Bender noted that while significant progress has been made in stabilizing the business, "important work" remains as they focus on operational improvements and modernizing processes for 2026.

title: Hoth Therapeutics reports preclinical data on obesity treatment
summary: Hoth Therapeutics (NASDAQ:HOTH) announced positive preclinical results for its GDNF treatment candidate in female mice with diet-induced metabolic dysfunction, demonstrating superior efficacy compared to semaglutide in restoring serum cholesterol and maintaining lower triglyceride levels. The company also highlighted its strong liquidity, bullish analyst outlook with a $5 price target, and recent positive interim results from its CLEER-001 clinical trial and a key patent approval in China for its HT-KIT cancer program.

title: Celsius (CELH) Stock Is Climbing Tuesday: What's Driving The Action?
summary: Celsius Holdings' (CELH) stock is performing strongly due to a positive Q4 earnings report that exceeded analyst expectations, with revenue surging 117%. The company also announced an international expansion into Spain through a partnership with Suntory Beverage & Food Spain. Analysts from Bank of America and Goldman Sachs have expressed bullish sentiment, with Bank of America upgrading CELH to Buy and raising its price target.

title: Carpenter Technology (CRS) Just Flashed Golden Cross Signal: Do You Buy?
summary: Carpenter Technology (CRS) recently showed a bullish signal by crossing above its 20-day moving average, suggesting a short-term upward trend. The stock has gained 8.4% in the last four weeks and holds a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold), supported by positive earnings estimate revisions. Investors may find CRS an attractive option for potential future gains due to these technical and fundamental indicators.

title: Philips connects patient monitoring and diagnostics to advance platform-based care intelligence at HIMSS26
summary: Royal Philips showcased at HIMSS26 how open, interoperable, AI-enabled platforms can connect continuous patient monitoring with diagnostic insights to improve care. The company highlighted its enterprise patient monitoring and Integrated Diagnostics capabilities, which aim to unify fragmented data across care settings and specialties. This approach helps healthcare professionals gain a more comprehensive understanding of patients, reduce workload, and facilitate earlier interventions for better outcomes.

title: More buildings for sale, but investors still bidding hard worldwide
summary: JLL reports that global investor bidding intensity across key property sectors (Multi-family, Industrial & Logistics, Retail, and Office) has converged to the narrowest spread in over three years, signaling a more normalized market in 2026. Despite an increase in properties for sale, buyer interest remains competitive, with multi-family leading, industrial rebounding, retail liquidity deepening, and office dynamics improving from late 2023 lows. JLL's Global Bid Intensity Index tracks these trends, noting that while the Middle East conflict introduces uncertainty, generally healthy economic fundamentals are expected to support an intensifying capital markets liquidity cycle in 2026.

title: Monday.Com Ltd (MNDY) Shares Down 3.1% on Mar 10
summary: Shares of Monday.Com Ltd (MNDY) fell 3.10% on March 10, closing at $78.54. Wall Street analysts have an average one-year target price of $136.80 for MNDY, implying a 74.19% upside, with a consensus "Outperform" rating. GuruFocus estimates the GF Value for MNDY at $389.99, suggesting an even greater upside of 396.58%.

title: Renn fund president Stahl buys shares worth $3,345
summary: Murray Stahl, President and Co-Portfolio Manager at RENN Fund, Inc. (RCG), has purchased 1,134 shares of the company's common stock for a total of $3,345 on March 9, 2026. This insider acquisition comes as the stock trades near its 52-week high, having achieved a 12.45% year-to-date return. The filing detailed both direct and indirect ownership of these shares through various entities.

title: Distribution Solutions Group (NASDAQ:DSGR) Price Target Lowered to $35.00 at Barrington Research
summary: Barrington Research has lowered its price target for Distribution Solutions Group (NASDAQ:DSGR) from $41.00 to $35.00, while maintaining an "outperform" rating. This adjustment follows the company's recent earnings miss, with shares currently trading near their one-year low. Despite the lowered target and mixed analyst ratings, management has authorized a $30 million stock buyback program.

title: Hotel101 Madrid now open – DoubleDragon
summary: DoubleDragon Corp.'s hospitality subsidiary, Hotel101 Global Holdings Corp., has officially opened Hotel101-Madrid, marking the first time a Filipino homegrown hotel brand operates outside the Philippines. The 680-room hotel in Madrid aims to generate recurring revenue and dollar inflows for the Philippine economy. Operating on an asset-light, prop-tech model, Hotel101 Global's long-term goal is to have one million rooms in 100 countries.

title: Hotel101 opens Madrid hotel
summary: Hotel101 Global Holdings Corp., a Nasdaq-listed subsidiary of DoubleDragon Corp., has opened its first European project, the 680-room Hotel101-Madrid in Spain. This marks a significant milestone as the first Filipino homegrown hotel brand to operate overseas. The hotel aims to generate recurring revenue as part of Hotel101 Global's expansion strategy, which includes operating one million rooms across 100 countries worldwide.

title: Hotel101 opens 680-room Madrid hotel
summary: DoubleDragon Corp.'s hospitality arm, Hotel101 Global Holdings Corp., has opened Hotel101-Madrid, marking its first European project and making it the first Filipino homegrown hotel brand to operate overseas. The 680-room hotel in Madrid's Valdebebas district offers "HappyRoom" units and four-star amenities, targeting both business and leisure travelers. Hotel101 also has properties in the Philippines, with ongoing developments in Japan and the US, and a second European project planned in Italy.

title: NICE Ltd (NICE) Shares Gap Down to $121.64 on Mar 10
summary: Shares of NICE Ltd (NICE) gapped down to $121.64 on March 10, a 3.18% drop from its previous close, with trading volume at 13.4% of its average daily volume. Despite the dip, analysts forecast an average target price of $155.21, suggesting a 27.60% upside, and GuruFocus estimates a fair value of $285.86, indicating a potential 135% upside. The company currently holds an "Outperform" status based on analyst recommendations.

title: Analysts Offer Insights on Consumer Cyclical Companies: Booking Holdings (BKNG) and Century Casinos (CNTY)
summary: Analysts have provided new ratings for Booking Holdings (BKNG) and Century Casinos (CNTY) within the Consumer Cyclical sector. Bernstein analyst Richard Clarke maintained a Hold rating on Booking Holdings with a $5407.00 price target, while Craig-Hallum's Ryan Sigdahl maintained a Buy rating on Century Casinos. The consensus rating for Booking Holdings is Strong Buy, with a Moderate Buy for Century Casinos.

title: M Scott Welch Buys 5,000 Shares of Lakeland Financial (NASDAQ:LKFN) Stock
summary: M. Scott Welch, a director at Lakeland Financial (NASDAQ:LKFN), recently purchased 5,000 shares of the company's stock for $277,250, increasing his total holdings to 54,000 shares. This transaction follows another purchase of 10,000 shares in February. The company recently reported strong quarterly earnings, beating analyst estimates, and increased its quarterly dividend to $0.52 per share.

title: LENZ: Rapidly growing prescriber base and strong refill rates highlight successful launch momentum
summary: A novel presbyopia eye drop from LENZ Therapeutics (LENZ) is experiencing a successful launch, marked by rapid and sustained efficacy, strong safety, and high adoption and refill rates. Early DTC campaigns and an expanding prescriber base are increasing awareness, with international expansion also underway. This success is evidenced by insights from the Leerink Global Healthcare Conference 2026.

title: TD Cowen says buy Rivian stock ahead of R2 launch
summary: TD Cowen has upgraded Rivian (RIVN) stock to "buy" ahead of its R2 SUV launch, pushing past its moving averages. Analyst Itay Michaeli believes the R2, priced around $45,000, will be a transformative volume driver, targeting the American compact SUV market and competing with Tesla Model Y. The upgrade also reflects confidence in Rivian's improving financial structure, with a $20 price target based on a narrower EBITDA loss and higher terminal multiple, anticipating profitability by 2027.

title: Cogent Communications (NASDAQ:CCOI) Shares Gap Down Following Insider Selling
summary: Cogent Communications (NASDAQ:CCOI) shares gapped down following insider selling from its VP, CFO, and a director, who collectively sold over 10,000 shares. The stock opened lower at $21.85 and traded around $21.69, down approximately 3.0%. This occurred despite the company topping EPS estimates in its latest earnings report, though revenue declined year-over-year and analysts remain mixed on the stock.

title: Sikorsky Collaborates with Robinson Helicopter Company to Integrate MATRIX™ Autonomy into Robinson R66 TURBINETRUCK
summary: Sikorsky, a Lockheed Martin company, has partnered with Robinson Helicopter Company to launch the R66 TURBINETRUCK, an autonomous cargo helicopter integrating Sikorsky's MATRIX™ autonomy system with Robinson Unmanned's new helicopter. This platform is designed for a variety of utility missions, including cargo delivery and resupply for both civil and military operators. The collaboration aims to extend the R66's reach into new mission sets, offering an affordable and reliable solution for critical asset transport in contested environments.

title: Himax Technologies Target of Unusually Large Options Trading (NASDAQ:HIMX)
summary: Himax Technologies (NASDAQ:HIMX) experienced unusually large options trading on Tuesday, with traders purchasing 16,488 call options, a 956% increase from the daily average. This activity coincided with a 7.4% jump in Himax's stock price to $8.30, despite analysts maintaining a "Hold" rating and an $8.00 target price. The company recently reported Q4 EPS in line with estimates and revenue slightly above expectations, setting Q1 2026 guidance for EPS to be between $0.020 and $0.040.

title: Cosmos Health Enters into LOI to Acquire $11,500,000 Pharmacy Distribution Network; Remains on Track to Deliver 2027 Revenue Guidance of $155,800,000
summary: Cosmos Health Inc. announced a Letter of Intent to acquire a pharmacy distribution network in Greece, generating approximately $11.5 million in annual revenue. This acquisition, part of the Company's strategic plan, is expected to expand CosmoFarm's commercial reach and enhance operating efficiencies through automated logistics. Cosmos Health remains focused on organic growth and disciplined acquisitions to achieve its 2027 revenue guidance of $155.8 million, aiming to strengthen its pharmaceutical distribution and increase shareholder value.

title: BioLife Solutions (NASDAQ:BLFS) Hits New 1-Year Low on Insider Selling
summary: BioLife Solutions (NASDAQ:BLFS) recently hit a new 52-week low after an insider, Karen A. Foster, sold 96,923 shares for approximately $1.95 million, reducing her stake by 46%. Despite this insider selling and the stock trading below its moving averages, analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating with an average target price of $32.00. The company, which specializes in biopreservation and cold chain solutions, has a market capitalization of $929.87 million and is largely held by institutional investors.

title: Esperion Therapeutics, Inc (ESPR) Reports Q4 Earnings
summary: Esperion Therapeutics, Inc. (ESPR) reported strong Q4 earnings, with $0.22 per share compared to a loss of $0.11 last year, and generated $168.4 million in revenue. The pharmaceutical company specializes in medicines like NEXLETOL and NEXLIZET for elevated LDL cholesterol. A detailed analysis is forthcoming on AlphaStreet.

title: ESPR Stock Falls After Esperion’s Mixed Q4 — Retail Remains Unfazed, Eyes ‘Massive’ Upcoming Catalysts
summary: Esperion Therapeutics (ESPR) shares fell over 6% after reporting mixed Q4 earnings, with EPS below Wall Street expectations despite higher revenue. Despite the dip, retail sentiment on Stocktwits remains highly bullish, anticipating significant upcoming catalysts. The company highlighted future growth plans including U.S. market penetration, advancements in its oral triple-combination program, and geographical expansion, along with strategic acquisitions to advance its Vision 2040.

title: Medline highlights top-performing distribution centers
summary: Medline has announced the recipients of its 2025 Distribution Center of the Year Award and Safety, Awareness for Everyone (SAFE) Award, recognizing its top-performing facilities across four tiers of size. These awards highlight the dedication of Medline's team members in maintaining high standards of safety, service, quality, and efficiency, enabling the company to provide next-day delivery to 95% of its U.S. customers. The initiative has been in place for over 20 years, acknowledging operational excellence and commitment to improving healthcare operations nationwide.

title: Manulife Keeps The Bulls Coming For Canada's Insurance Giant, As Margins Impress (NYSE:MFC)
summary: Manulife Financial (MFC) has received a reaffirmed buy rating due to its strong financial performance, impressive margins, and robust capital. The company benefits from a diversified insurance and wealth management model, evidenced by recent acquisitions, organic insurance growth, and a compelling dividend increase. Despite some challenges like net outflows in wealth management, MFC's profitability and low debt-to-equity ratio position it favorably against peers, supporting a bullish outlook.

title: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC Urges Ramaco Resources, Inc. Investors to Act: Class Action Filed Alleging Investor Harm
summary: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC has filed a class action lawsuit against Ramaco Resources, Inc. alleging that the company made false and misleading statements regarding the development progress of its Brook Mine. The lawsuit claims that Ramaco (NASDAQ: METC) overstated mining activity and failed to disclose that no active work was taking place at the mine, thereby harming investors. Investors who purchased Ramaco securities between July 31, 2025, and October 23, 2025, are encouraged to join the lawsuit, with a lead plaintiff deadline of March 31, 2026.

title: Richard Bernstein Advisors Trims UnitedHealth Group Holdings
summary: Richard Bernstein Advisors LLC significantly reduced its investment in UnitedHealth Group Incorporated during the third quarter, selling 12,100 shares and decreasing its stake by 60%. The firm now holds 8,060 shares valued at $2.783 million. This move by a major institutional investor could signal shifts in market sentiment regarding the healthcare giant.

title: Elong Power (Nasdaq: ELPW) implements 1-for-80 share consolidation March 12
summary: Elong Power (Nasdaq: ELPW) is implementing a 1-for-80 share consolidation for its Class A and Class B ordinary shares, effective March 12, 2026, to comply with Nasdaq's minimum bid price requirement. This consolidation will significantly reduce the number of outstanding shares and increase their par value, with fractional shares being rounded up. The company's Class A ordinary shares will continue to trade under the symbol ELPW but with a new CUSIP.

title: High Oil Prices & a 38% 1-Year Rally: Is ExxonMobil a Buy?
summary: ExxonMobil has seen a significant 37.8% rally over the past year, outperforming its industry and competitors, driven by high oil prices and strong upstream assets in the Permian and Guyana. While its robust balance sheet and dividend-paying history are attractive, the stock is currently considered overvalued compared to the broader industry and some peers, leading to a "Hold" recommendation from Zacks Investment Research. Investors who already own the stock are advised to hold onto it, but new investment is not recommended at its current valuation.

title: Stocks making big moves yesterday: United Airlines, 3D Systems, Redwire, Sprout Social, and MACOM
summary: This article details the stock movements of several companies on Monday, March 9, 2026. United Airlines and Sprout Social saw declines due to rising fuel costs and geopolitical tensions, while 3D Systems, Redwire, and MACOM experienced gains after positive financial results, analyst upgrades, and increased demand in their sectors.

title: Does The J. M. Smucker (SJM) Share Price Disconnect From Its Cash Flow Outlook
summary: This article analyzes J. M. Smucker's (SJM) share price against its cash flow outlook using two valuation approaches. A Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests the stock is significantly undervalued by 59.0%, while a Price-to-Sales (P/S) comparison indicates it is priced about right relative to its fair ratio. The article encourages investors to consider different narratives and assumptions when evaluating the company's worth.

title: Occidental Petroleum falls 3.1% as oil prices reverse lower amid Middle East-driven volatility
summary: Occidental Petroleum's stock (OXY) dropped 3.1% due to a sharp pullback in crude oil prices, which reversed lower after a war-driven spike. This volatility is attributed to broader sector movements and profit-taking rather than specific company negative news, despite recent positive announcements like a dividend increase and the sale of its chemical business. The company's high oil-price sensitivity makes it a common vehicle for traders to adjust exposure quickly when crude prices fluctuate.
